title: Dealer
description: **About Us** At Davy, it's the unique talents of all our people that have been the foundations of our success for 100 years. As we continue to grow, so do you. Because you are not just part of our team \- you are a key player in shaping our future. At Davy, you are the difference. Established in 1926, the Davy Group sits at the heart of wealth and capital in Ireland, providing a broad range of services to high\-net\-worth individuals, self\-directed investors, small\-to\-medium enterprises, credit unions, corporations, and domestic \& international institutional investors. Our culture is shaped around three values, which are at the heart of everything we do: client success, one Davy, and building a proud legacy. Sharing the same goals, we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for our clients. As part of a team of over 900, you will be encouraged to learn and to grow, both professionally and personally. **About The Role** An exciting opportunity has opened up in our Davy Select division of Davy. In the role, the successful candidate will: * Executing trades received from various channels (Phone/Post/Email/Secure Comms) and ensuring adherence to Best Execution * Ensuring clients are consistently provided with excellent dealing services * Assist clients with Corporate Actions, Stock Set Ups and other queries * Liaising with clients and intermediary brokers, dealing promptly and efficiently with queries * Provide support to clients using our myDavy portal * Participate in outstanding balance projects as required * Being proactive in identifying existing and new execution only clients interested in and/or suitable for referral to other teams within the Davy Group; * Participate in team projects as required * Comply with the Risk and Compliance frameworks, policies and procedures associated with the role * Willing to work Late shift (1pm\-9pm) occasionally **Requirements** **About you** We are looking for somebody who has the following background and skills: * Degree level in a Business related discipline; * APA Savings and Investments qualification essential; * A strong client focus to ensure effective service delivery; * Demonstrable interest in investment; * Proven team\-working skills; * Very strong communication and presentation skills; verbal and written; * Excellent administration and IT skills, including a sound understanding of MS Office; * An ability to prioritise high value\-added tasks and meet tight deadlines; **Desirable** * Masters level in a Business\-related discipline; * Full QFA Qualification **What We Offer** At Davy, our people are our biggest investment. In today's fast\-moving world, we know it's important to feel secure and empowered. That's why Davy benefits support you through all of life's stages, offering health and wellness, flexible working options, flexible benefits allowance, learning and development opportunities, through to assisted retirement planning. **Health and Wellbeing** Psychological safety, emotional wellbeing and support are a priority at Davy. We offer a comprehensive Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) and mental health first aid. Our Sports \& Social teams promote physical wellbeing and social connection. **Reward and Recognition** We offer a competitive remuneration package to include salary, bonus, pension and a host of additional benefits to protect you and your family when it matters most. **Growth and Development** Our award\-winning L\&D function supports you and your growth at Davy. We offer a comprehensive range of professional and personal development opportunities. Our culture supports learning, and we encourage you to explore more at Davy. **Positive Workplace** Our workplace is inclusive, diverse, and offers everyone a sense of belonging. We are flexible and continue to support a hybrid working model. You will get to be your best at Davy. **Values and Culture** Exceptional client outcomes delivered by a world class team, underpinned by our core Davy values \- One Team, Client Success, and Proud Legacy. **Important Information** The position set out above is defined in the Fitness \& Probity Standards issued by the Central Bank of Ireland ("Fitness \& Probity Standards") as a "Controlled Function (CF)", namely CF4\. The role is also subject to the Minimum Competency Code. In compliance with the Fitness and Probity Standards, any offer of employment is conditional upon you demonstrating that you meet the Standards." Davy is an equal opportunities employer and we are committed to fostering an inclusive workplace. We value diversity both in background and in experience. You might not meet all of the requirements outlined in the above job description, but we would still like to hear from you. We review all applications received. Appointment to this role is subject to the candidate's eligibility to work in Ireland. Where agency assistance is required, our Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to us by recruitment agencies will not be accepted for this role. Davy Group is a member of the Bank of Ireland Group.

title: Deputy Financial Controller
description: **Role Overview** Reporting to the Financial Controller, the Deputy Financial Controller plays a central role in the day\-to\-day management, reporting, and control of the organisation’s finances. The postholder deputises for the Financial Controller as required and helps drive accuracy, efficiency, and strategic insight across the business. This is an excellent opportunity for a fully ACCA qualified finance professional to develop their career in a dynamic and commercially focused environment. **Key Responsibilities** **Financial Reporting \& Control** • Prepare accurate monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements in line with applicable accounting standards. • Produce management accounts, variance analysis, and commentary for senior stakeholders. • Own the integrity of the general ledger and deliver a timely, accurate month\-end close. • Maintain and continually strengthen financial controls, policies, and procedures. **Budgeting \& Forecasting** • Lead the cashflow forecasting process, producing short and long\-term forecasts, flagging liquidity risks, and presenting findings to senior leadership. • Support the annual budget and reforecast cycles, partnering with budget holders across the business. • Deliver financial analysis and scenario modelling to inform business planning and decision\-making. • Monitor budget performance and surface risks and opportunities to the Financial Controller. **Compliance \& Audit** • Coordinate the external audit, acting as a primary point of contact for auditors. • Ensure compliance with statutory and regulatory reporting requirements, including VAT and corporation tax. • Support the implementation of robust internal audit and risk management frameworks. **Leadership \& Process Improvement** • Supervise and mentor junior members of the finance team, fostering continuous improvement and professional development. • Deputise for the Financial Controller, providing leadership and direction to the wider finance function. • Identify and implement improvements to financial systems, reporting tools, and processes, ensuring data integrity and appropriate access controls. **Person Specification** **Essential** • Fully ACCA qualified, with 1–3 years’ post\-qualification experience in a senior finance role. • Proven track record in management accounts, financial reporting, and budgeting. • Demonstrated ability to manage and develop a finance team. • High level of accuracy and attention to detail, with strong analytical and problem\-solving skills. • Excellent communication skills, able to present financial information clearly to non\-finance audiences. • Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and experience with accounting and ERP systems. **Desirable** • Experience in the property or real estate sector. • Exposure to external audit coordination and statutory accounts preparation. • Working knowledge of VAT returns and corporation tax compliance. • Experience with financial systems implementation or process improvement projects. • Familiarity with applying artificial intelligence tools in a finance context. **What We Offer** • Competitive salary commensurate with experience and qualifications. • Generous annual leave plus bank holidays. • Hybrid and flexible working arrangements (role dependent). • Continuous professional development and clear career progression. • A supportive, collaborative, and ambitious working environment.

title: DevOps Engineer
description: **DevOps Engineers \| Kubernetes, AWS, Azure \& GCP \| Multi\-Cloud Platform Build** * Dublin Docklands, 4 days onsite, 1 day from home * €70,000 \- €110,000 base \+ bonus Our client is a global software company that has been delivering mission\-critical technology platforms for more than 45 years across healthcare, finance, logistics and government sectors. Their systems underpin large\-scale environments where uptime, scalability, resilience and security are essential. As part of a major cloud transformation programme, they’re building out a brand\-new DevOps and cloud platform engineering function in Dublin and are hiring multiple engineers across Senior, Lead and Principal\-level roles. This team will focus on designing, automating and operating large\-scale Kubernetes and cloud\-native infrastructure across AWS, Azure and GCP, supporting globally distributed SaaS and managed service platforms. Depending on your level, you’ll either be deeply hands\-on across automation, Kubernetes and infrastructure engineering, or operating as a senior technical leader shaping architecture, engineering standards, cloud strategy and long\-term platform scalability. The environment is heavily engineering\-led with a strong focus on Kubernetes at scale, Infrastructure as Code, observability, automation, platform reliability and modern DevOps practices across highly available production systems. **Non\-Negotiables** * Kubernetes in large\-scale production environments * DevOps or Platform Engineering experience across AWS, Azure and/or GCP * Terraform and Infrastructure as Code * Helm and Kubernetes package management * Strong Linux and containerisation experience * CI/CD pipelines and automation tooling * Kubernetes networking, security and observability * Experience operating highly available cloud platforms * Strong troubleshooting skills across Kubernetes and cloud environments * Experience working in complex enterprise or mission\-critical environments **What You’ll Work With** * Kubernetes * AWS / Azure / GCP * EKS / AKS / GKE * Terraform * Helm * GitOps workflows * ArgoCD / Flux * Prometheus * Grafana * Loki * Fluentd / Fluent Bit * RBAC and secrets management * Multi\-cloud and hybrid infrastructure * CI/CD and platform automation **Nice to Haves** * CKA / CKAD / CKS certifications * Service mesh technologies such as Istio, Linkerd or Cilium * Velero, Kasten or Kubernetes backup tooling * Spectro Cloud or enterprise Kubernetes platforms * SRE and reliability engineering experience * Experience mentoring engineers or setting technical standards * Open\-source Kubernetes or cloud\-native contributions * Experience supporting regulated or compliance\-heavy environments **Why Join** You’ll be joining during a significant investment into cloud\-native infrastructure and platform engineering, helping build and scale Kubernetes platforms that support critical global systems used across healthcare and other highly regulated industries. There’s strong technical ownership throughout the team. Senior engineers will influence automation, DevOps tooling and platform standards, while Principal\-level hires will shape architecture, cloud strategy and long\-term engineering direction across multi\-cloud environments. The Dublin engineering function is scaling rapidly, making this a strong opportunity for progression, visibility and the chance to help define how the DevOps and platform capability evolves over the coming years **DevOps Engineers \| Kubernetes, AWS, Azure \& GCP \| Multi\-Cloud Platform Build**

title: Drug Product Associate, Sterile Drug Product
description: **Working with Us** Challenging. Meaningful. Life\-changing. Those aren't words that are usually associated with a job. But working at Bristol Myers Squibb is anything but usual. Here, uniquely interesting work happens every day, in every department. From optimizing a production line to the latest breakthroughs in cell therapy, this is work that transforms the lives of patients, and the careers of those who do it. You'll get the chance to grow and thrive through opportunities uncommon in scale and scope, alongside high\-achieving teams. Take your career farther than you thought possible. Bristol Myers Squibb recognizes the importance of balance and flexibility in our work environment. We offer a wide variety of competitive benefits, services and programs that provide our employees with the resources to pursue their goals, both at work and in their personal lives. Read more careers.bms.com/working\-with\-us . BMS Cruiserath Sterile Drug Product is seeking an experienced individual with manufacturing experience to provide on the floor technical support to our manufacturing teams within our new Sterile Drug Product (SDP) facility. Reporting to the Senior Manager, Manufacturing Shift Lead, the Drug Product Associate will play a key role in the establishing our multi\-product commercial Drug Product Facility with both vial and syringe fill finish technology. The **Drug Product Associate** will provide input and support all phases of the SDP project, such as, CQV, Site Operational Readiness, Technical Transfer, new product introduction and commercialization activities. Post start\-up this role will be embedded within the shift teams charged with delivering the manufacturing plan for the facility. The start\-up nature of this project will require adaptability \& innovation due to the multiple project phases and multiple products onsite. The role will be both challenging \& rewarding and will thus ensure that the successful candidate can seize a wide range of development opportunities within BMS. * The position will commence on a day schedule, with a planned transition to shift work in the future **Key Responsibilities** * Support the execution of one or more processes that include Formulation, Filling (vial \& syringe), Lyophilization, Capping, Visual Inspection \& Primary Packaging * Support technical issue troubleshooting, resolution both during and post facility start\-up. * Work with the cross functional departments to facilitate the implementation of the project design and validation of the SDP manufacturing facility. * Support all phases of Commissioning / Qualification and Validation during Start\-up phase of state\-of\-the\-art Drug Product Facility * Support all Capital / Tech Transfer / Operational Readiness activities to support Commercial Readiness * Support delivery of training to SDP team on day\-to\-day operations within area of responsibility * Right First\-Time execution of commercial manufacturing processes according to established work instructions * Support the implementation of all key Operational Excellence initiatives (5S / Std Work / LSW / Kaizen / Problem Solving etc.) * Taking part in investigations and optimization of processes using scientific, engineering and lean principles * Proficient use of process automation systems * Assisting with review of batch and exception reports for manufacturing lots in conjunction with Quality Assurance representative * Engages in and supports culture of continuous improvement initiatives and safe working practices. * Designee for Senior DPA as required. * Support any other business deliverables as needed. **Qualifications \& Experience** * Experience in a high\-speed manufacturing / regulated environment essential * Qualification in science, engineering, or related subject desirable. * Expertise in sterile drug product manufacturing along with demonstrated proficiency in technical troubleshooting and resolution is advantageous but not essential. * Ability to flexibly adapt to changing business needs in a start\-up environment. **Why you should apply** * You will help patients in their fight against serious diseases * You will be part of a company that encourages excellence and innovation, respects diversity, develops leaders and values its employees. * You'll get a competitive salary and a great benefits package including an annual bonus, pension contribution, family medical allowance, 27 days of annual leave, life assurance and on\-site gym If you come across a role that intrigues you but doesn't perfectly line up with your resume, we encourage you to apply anyway. You could be one step away from work that will transform your life and career. **Uniquely Interesting Work, Life\-changing Careers** With a single vision as inspiring as Transforming patients' lives through science™ , every BMS employee plays an integral role in work that goes far beyond ordinary. Each of us is empowered to apply our individual talents and unique perspectives in a supportive culture, promoting global participation in clinical trials, while our shared values of passion, innovation, urgency, accountability, inclusion and integrity bring out the highest potential of each of our colleagues. **On\-site Protocol** BMS has an occupancy structure that determines where an employee is required to conduct their work. This structure includes site\-essential, site\-by\-design, field\-based and remote\-by\-design jobs. The occupancy type that you are assigned is determined by the nature and responsibilities of your role Site\-essential roles require 100% of shifts onsite at your assigned facility. Site\-by\-design roles may be eligible for a hybrid work model with at least 50% onsite at your assigned facility. For these roles, onsite presence is considered an essential job function and is critical to collaboration, innovation, productivity, and a positive Company culture. For field\-based and remote\-by\-design roles the ability to physically travel to visit customers, patients or business partners and to attend meetings on behalf of BMS as directed is an essential job function. **Supporting People With Disabilities** BMS is dedicated to ensuring that people with disabilities can excel through a transparent recruitment process, reasonable workplace accommodations/adjustments and ongoing support in their roles. Applicants can request a reasonable workplace accommodation/adjustment prior to accepting a job offer. If you require reasonable accommodations/adjustments in completing this application, or in any part of the recruitment process, direct your inquiries to adastaffingsupport@bms.com . Visit careers.bms.com/ eeo \-accessibility to access our complete Equal Employment Opportunity statement. **Candidate Rights** BMS will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records, pursuant to applicable laws in your area. If you live in or expect to work from Los Angeles County if hired for this position, please visit this page for important additional information https //careers.bms.com/california\-residents/ **Data Protection** We will never request payments, financial information, or social security numbers during our application or recruitment process. Learn more about protecting yourself at https //careers.bms.com/fraud\-protection . Any data processed in connection with role applications will be treated in accordance with applicable data privacy policies and regulations. If you believe that the job posting is missing information required by local law or incorrect in any way, please contact BMS at TAEnablement@bms.com . Please provide the Job Title and Requisition number so we can review. Communications related to your application should not be sent to this email and you will not receive a response. Inquiries related to the status of your application should be directed to Chat with Ripley. R1602498 Drug Product Associate, Sterile Drug Product

title: Dynamics CE & Power Platform Software Developer
description: We have an exciting Dynamics CE \& Power Platform role with a top company in Dublin. This company offer a great work/ life balance, hybrid remote work and top packages. Experience * Have a broad knowledge of the Power Platform technology stack. * Design, develop, and implement Power Platform solutions to meet business needs * Develop custom connectors and integrations to connect with external systems * Build, test, and deploy Power Apps model\-driven apps * Create and configure Power Automate workflows * Work collaboratively with stakeholders to gather requirements, provide guidance, and communicate project status * Ensure solutions are scalable, maintainable, and adhere to industry best practices * Troubleshoot and debug issues, and provide technical support when needed * Mentor and train junior team members Requirements: * At least 3 years of experience in developing solutions using Power Platform * Strong knowledge of Power Apps and Power Automate * Experience building custom connectors and integrations * Experiencing utilising Azure Resources (ADF, Logic Apps, Functions) would be a plus * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills * Ability to work independently and as part of a team * Proven ability to lead and mentor junior developers * Microsoft Power Platform certifications are a plus For more info you can contact me at david@db\-recruitment.ie

title: E-Commerce Order Fulfilment Associate - Bookshop
description: We are a growing, fast paced company looking for the right individual to help us continue to expand our E\-Commerce presence. The E\-Commerce Order Fulfilment Associate supports the online operations of our bookshop by ensuring every order is packed, shipped, and delivered with accuracy and care. This role plays a key part in maintaining our shop’s reputation for excellent service ensuring that customers receive their books quickly and in perfect condition. You’ll manage online orders, track inventory, and assist in creating a smooth connection between our online and in\-store experiences. **Key Responsibilities** * Process online book orders through our ecommerce platform with the use of an RF scanner. * Pick, inspect, and carefully pack books and other merchandise for shipment. * Print shipping labels and ensure timely dispatch via carriers. * Maintain organised shelves and up\-to\-date online inventory. * Communicate with customers or staff regarding backorders, special requests, or shipping issues. * Monitor order tracking and proactively resolve any delivery delays or errors. * Communicate with management about product restocks and updating product listings, descriptions, or inventory counts online. * Support seasonal sales, pre\-orders, and signed edition releases. * Collaborate with the retail team to coordinate in\-store pickups or local deliveries. * Maintain a tidy and efficient fulfilment workspace. **Qualifications** * 1\+ year experience in ecommerce, retail, or warehouse fulfilment (bookstore experience a plus). * Strong attention to detail \- especially when handling book editions, ISBNs, and order accuracy. * Comfortable using ecommerce systems and shipping tools. * Excellent organisational and time management skills. * Ability to work on your feet for extended periods. * Friendly, team\-oriented attitude and passion for books and readers. **Preferred Skills** * Experience with Shopify, WaveOMS and Booksolve. * Knowledge of basic inventory management principles. * Interest in literature, publishing, or independent book retail. * Experience with sustainable or gift packaging practices. **Performance Indicators** * Order accuracy and on\-time shipment rate. * Inventory accuracy and fulfilment efficiency. * Customer satisfaction and feedback ratings. * Reduced packaging waste and improved efficiency. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €14\.15 per hour Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Employee discount Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Electrical Technician
description: **Req ID:** 502685 **Location: Dublin** **Appointment Basis: Permanent** **Apply by: 28/05/2026** **Salary: circa** **€50,000 \-** **€60,000 DOE plus shift allowance** At Alstom, we understand transport networks and what moves people. From high\-speed trains, metros, monorails, and trams, to turnkey systems, services, infrastructure, signalling and digital mobility, we offer our diverse customers the broadest portfolio in the industry. Every day, 80,000 colleagues lead the way to greener and smarter mobility worldwide, connecting cities as we reduce carbon and replace cars. Alstom is the UK \& Ireland’s leading supplier of new trains and train services, and a leading signalling and rail infrastructure provider. Alstom have built, or are building, just under 40% of the UK mainline train fleet; as well as the entire fleets in service with London Underground and Dublin Luas. We provide the widest range of smart solutions in the rail market, from innovative high\-speed rolling stock, metros and trams to maintenance, modernisation, infrastructure and signalling. **Your future role** Take on a new challenge and apply your Electrical systems knowledge as a technical team member in a new cutting\-edge field. You’ll report to Train manager and work alongside our active and highly resourceful colleagues. You’ll be able to demonstrate your unique attributes and skills. Day\-to\-day, you’ll work closely with teams across the business, carry out testing and commissioning activities on dart\+ trains in accordance with pre\-written test and commissioning procedures and much more. You’ll specifically take care of all coupling activities, static and dynamic testing and warranty issues and modifications. We’ll look to you for: * Develop and increase knowledge of all Dart\+ train functions and sub\-systems. * Troubleshooting of all warranty defects and provide technical assistance on TSSSA contract. * Upload software and downloads to carry out critical asset repairs and upgrades. * Apply special processes as crimping and torque tightening. * Functionally test train borne systems with minimal supervision using a variety of techniques and methods, including the use of computer\-based diagnostics systems. * Provide technical support to the depot teams in the provision of fault\-finding expertise and assistance with train repair and train maintenance. * Undertake test runs to assist in fault finding and resolution of faults, in particular the close\-out of repeat failures. * Assistance in component change outs, and failure reporting for ESG and reliability growth teams * Liaise with the ESG and specialist engineers as necessary, gathering technical information needed to enable technical problems with trains, procedures, materials or component problems to be resolved. * Carry out all duties in accordance with the Traincare Centre quality and safety procedures. * Driving Company vehicles for Depot operational or Technical related work such as breakdowns or occasional travel to do other work at other sites, this would only be requested of current eligible and authorised Technicians as agreed with relevant site TCM \& HRM. * Carrying out overhead isolations as required. * Topping up of sanding systems of rail vehicles * General depot duties e.g.: Line of Routes * Static / functional and performance tests * Review and rectify test specifications and documentation for functional tests. * Continuously improve processes and procedures. * Coach and develop other Technicians as required. * To record all Service Orders all activities and time taken to complete tasks. * Attend regular team briefings and communicate any relevant matters. * Report accidents and incidents through the appropriate channels **All about you** We value passion and attitude over experience. That’s why we don’t expect you to have every single skill. Instead, we’ve listed some that we think will help you succeed and grow in this role: * Qualified in a Technical Discipline – Qualified Electrician FETAC Level 6 National Craft Certificate (NCC) is required for this role * Experience of electronics, hydraulics, pneumatics and mechanics. * Experience of working within a process / production environment. * Understanding work instructions and following procedures. * Ability to read detailed electrical drawings and cable schedules. * Applying electrical modifications * To inform the files of progress of modifications * Ability to fault find/diagnose issues on installations while testing. * Be adaptable and flexible. * Working knowledge of quality and health and safety standards and systems preferable * Crimping and Torque Tightening. * Efficient in Mechanical and electrical coupling. * Uploading of software. * IT literate with some knowledge of Microsoft software packages. * Good organisational skills. **Things you’ll enjoy** Join us on a life\-long transformative journey – the rail industry is here to stay, so you can grow and develop new skills and experiences throughout your career. You’ll also: * Enjoy stability, challenges and a long\-term career free from boring daily routines * Work with Alstom to deliver innovative and sustainable transport systems. * Collaborate with transverse teams and helpful colleagues * Contribute to innovative projects * Utilise Alstom’s essential values \- Agile, Inclusive and Responsible. These values are the driving force for Alstom employees in their working lives working environment. * Steer your career in whatever direction you choose across functions and countries * Benefit from our investment in your development, through award\-winning learning * Advancement in career progression and career growth * Benefit from a fair and dynamic reward package that recognises your performance and potential, plus comprehensive and competitive social coverage (life, medical, pension) You don’t need to be a train enthusiast to thrive with us. We guarantee that when you step onto one of our trains with your friends or family, you’ll be proud. If you’re up for the challenge, we’d love to hear from you! **What we offer** We offer a highly competitive remuneration package, days annual leave, plus bank holidays, with the opportunity to buy or sell holiday. Contributed healthcare, life assurance, an excellent company\-contributed pension scheme, and a wide range of flexible benefits that you can tailor to suit your lifestyle. Alstom is the leading company in the mobility sector, solving the most interesting challenges for tomorrow’s mobility. That’s why we value inquisitive and innovative people who are passionate about working together to reinvent mobility, making it smarter and more sustainable. Day after day, we are building an agile, inclusive, and responsible culture, where a diverse group of people are offered opportunities to learn, grow and advance in their careers, with options across functions and geographic locations. Are you ready to join a truly international community of great people on a challenging journey with a tangible impact and purpose? **Equal opportunity statement:** Alstom is an equal opportunity employer committed to creating an inclusive working environment where all our employees are encouraged to reach their full potential, and individual differences are valued and respected. All qualified applicants are considered for employment without regard to race, colour, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, national origin, disability status, or any other characteristic protected by local law. As a 'Disability Confident' employer, we will interview all disabled job applicants who match the essential criteria of the job description or specification. We will consider flexible working requests for all roles unless operational requirements prevent otherwise. \#LI\-AL2 **Job Segment:** Testing, Technician, Bank, Banking, Technical Support, Technology, Finance

title: Electrical Technician Pharma
description: **Location: Dublin 22** **Duration: Permanent** **Hours: 39 per week (day role)** This position will be based on a biopharmaceutical site on behalf of our client, a multinational and multicultural company which provides great career progression opportunities to the right person. **Overview:** The Electrical Utilities Technician is responsible for the execution of the preventive and corrective maintenance program as per the Client/PPU requirements. He/she will work as part of the Maintenance and Calibration team on a major client Biopharmaceutical site. He/she will liaise with the contract team, client engineering and equipment vendors to ensure maximum uptime of all utilities, plant and equipment, and that equipment is in compliance with all regulatory, statutory and client corporate requirements. **Duties include:** * Carry out work as per schedule supplied by the Maintenance Coordinator, Planner and as per site policies, schedules and procedures. * Troubleshoot problems with utilities equipment as necessary and as directed by site supervisors. * Complete site documentation before and after work tasks, as appropriate. * Flexibility to work on equipment in all buildings. * Work and interact with the site DES team. * Flexibility to work around Maintenance Shutdown’s. * Be familiar with all GMP rules and regulations. * Be familiar with Statutory requirements. * Liaise and work with the Shift/Day Maintenance Team on all Utilities Equipment. * Liaise with Contractors during planned service maintenance. * Participate in the appropriate training. * Carry out Risk Assessment of Equipment Maintenance Task List steps and ensure these are always kept valid. * Update MTL’s as appropriate. * Carry out Periodic reviews of EWI/SOP to ensure documents are always current and valid. * All work on site to be carried out in accordance with site EHS (Environmental Health and Safety) and company EHS rules and guidelines. * Be responsible for ITP and ensure missing requirements are complete. * Participate in Audits of the Mechanical rooms and follow up on actions. * Ensure all tools and equipment are kept in good condition. * Day\-to\-day relationship with company’s Contract Manager and contract team. * Day\-to\-day relationship with client Engineering manager and representatives. * Manage Technical investigations for utility operations group and implement changes that occur from these investigations. * Complete Data Build on new equipment (create maintenance plans, preparing BOM, SOP’s, advising on rounds and readings for operation). * Evaluate Electrical PM job plan content and frequency. Recommend and implement improvements to optimise utility reliability, availability, and compliance. * Continuously evaluate equipment maintenance and failure history. Determine appropriateness of PM content and frequency. * Execute PM program along with the rest of the team. * Manage a program to measure and improve the effectiveness of the electrical PM program. Establish and evaluate effectiveness metrics to identify areas for improvement. * Supporting site contract maintenance team in minimizing downtime and risk of downtime to facility * Trouble\-shooting and investigation of utility plant equipment faults and failures along with the Shift Operation Group, this includes the Fire Alarm Detection system. * Introduction and installation of Reliability Centred Maintenance on all existing and future equipment. * Updating of utilities related standard operating procedures. * Identifying, liaising with, and, where appropriate, co\-ordination of third party contractors and suppliers on site. * Liaising with site projects team as clients engineering representative. * Liaising with site contract team to identify suppliers and quantities of spare parts; review and updating of spare part inventory of spares in clients site store. * Liaising with contract team and client to review maintenance programme, and effecting changes to methods and procedures where appropriate. * Continuous improvement of contract delivery on site. * Coordinating with personnel on utility requirements upon purchase of new equipment, movement of equipment, technology transfer etc. * Identifying and implementing, where appropriate, energy saving schemes. * Liaise with Quality and external auditing bodies upon audits. * Ensuring Engineering areas, in liaison with Contract Manager and Contract co\-ordinator, facilities and equipment are kept in a clean, safe and serviceable condition, and general housekeeping high standards are maintained. * Manage the execution of the utilities maintenance program and organisation structure in order to achieve and sustain optimum reliability, maintainability, useful life, and life cycle cost for utilities assets. * Implement and participate in utilities reliability programs as per the clients Global Reliability Program. * Drive local reviews of maintenance performance data using published maintenance excellence KPI results, areas that will be reviewed and improvements sought: \- Spares cost \- High levels of paperwork \- PM:CM ratio \- Maintenance man\-hours per equipment/system \- MTBF \- MTTR \- Equipment reliability trends * PM program content is to be reviewed on regular basis to remove non\-value added activities’ and duplication. * Develop long term (5 year) life cycle costing for asset replacement plans (equipment moving towards end of life). * Ensure that the CMMS system contains the most up to date information regarding equipment data, spares and maintenance man\-hours. * Maximise maintenance wrench time utilisation through effective planning. **Required skills:** * Electrical experience, 2 years minimum and good understanding of the basic fundamentals of the various utilities systems. * Pumps, HVAC Systems, Compressors, Boilers, steam systems and ancillary services, (DHW skids), Chillers, Cooling Towers, Clean steam Boilers, Purified Water Systems, CIP systems, Low /Medium voltage Electrical Systems. * Building Management Systems (Scada). * Good understanding of Preventive Maintenance Systems. * PLC’s. * Work within a Computerized Maintenance Management Work Order system environment. * Experience with Facility Start Up. * Work within a documented Permit to Work System. * Computer literate. * Ability to read and understand Electrical Drawings. * Understanding of SOP’s and GMP Engineering systems. * Understanding of Basic Instrumentation. * Must be able to demonstrate hands on experience with detailed knowledge/skill in the following area’s: * Troubleshooting / repair of Low \& medium Voltage Electrical Systems * Good problem solving and trouble shooting ability of motors/VFD’s, panels and Electrical safety systems * Vast knowledge of single and three phase systems (Motors) * Knowledge of Mechanical systems * Team Player

title: Electrical, Controls and Instrumentation Technician
description: **About Us** OFS Global Energy Solutions is a leading provider of integrated energy and engineering services, delivering reliable, efficient, and sustainable solutions across the power and oil \& gas sectors. Due to strong growth, we are currently recruiting for an **Electrical, Controls \& Instrumentation Technician** for a key Dublin based project, who will play a vital role in ensuring the reliability, safety, and efficiency of power generation systems. **Position Overview** Reporting to our Operations \& Maintenance Manager, this role involves hands\-on maintenance, supervision where required, and coordination of electrical and control systems, contributing directly to the uptime and resilience of mission\-critical infrastructure. **Primary Responsibilities:** **Maintenance Execution** * Performing day\-to\-day maintenance activities on electrical and control systems, including generators, switchgear, instrumentation, and automation systems. * Implementing the preventive and corrective maintenance program as per operational standards and manufacturer guidelines. **CMMS Management** * Ensuring the Computerized Maintenance Management System (CMMS) is accurately updated with all maintenance activities, work orders, and equipment status. * Using CMMS data to support planning, reporting, and continuous improvement initiatives. **Team Coordination** * Managing daily activities ensuring tasks are completed safely and efficiently. * Providing technical guidance and support to junior staff and contractors. **Spare Parts and Inventory** * Maintaining inventory records and ensure critical spares are stocked appropriately. **Documentation and Reporting** * Maintaining detailed records of all maintenance activities, inspections, and repairs. * Producing regular reports on equipment performance, maintenance trends, and compliance metrics. **Vendor and OEM Coordination** * Coordinating major maintenance activities with OEMs (e.g., Wartsila) and external vendors. * Ensuring third\-party work is performed to required standards and within agreed timelines. **Site Cybersecurity** * Ensuring cybersecurity protocols are followed for all control systems and instrumentation. * Monitoring and report any anomalies or vulnerabilities in the plant’s control network. **Qualifications \& Experience Required:** * Diploma or degree in Electrical Engineering, Instrumentation, or a related technical field. * Minimum of 3–5 years of experience in electrical and instrumentation maintenance, preferably in power generation or industrial plant environments. * Demonstrated experience working with HV/LV switchgear, including operation, isolation, maintenance and fault finding. * Electrical safety certification (e.g., Safe Electrical Work Practices). * Cybersecurity awareness training for industrial control systems is a plus. * Proficiency in troubleshooting and maintaining electrical systems, PLCs, SCADA, and instrumentation. * Familiarity with CMMS platforms and maintenance planning tools. * Understanding of generator systems, switchgear, and control panels. * Strong organizational and communication skills. * Ability to work independently and lead small teams. * Attention to detail and commitment to safety and fully understand and implement isolations where required * Full working rights in Ireland at the time of application. **Why Join OFS Global Energy Solutions?** At OFS Global Energy Solutions, we value innovation, safety, and teamwork. You will be part of a dynamic environment that offers growth opportunities, technical development, and the chance to contribute to world\-class energy operations. **OFS provide a comprehensive benefits package reflecting our commitment to our people.** **How to apply \- Please click on the relevant links attaching your current resume for immediate consideration.**

title: Electrician Maintenance technician
description: CBRE Global Workplace Solutions, leading global provider of integrated facilities and corporate real estate management, are recruiting an Electrical maintenance technician to join the team located **in Dublin 1\.** To carry out planned preventative maintenance and reactive works to client Electrical, HVAC \& building service Systems in accordance with agreed service levels. **Primary Objectives And Tasks** * This Role is primarily 5 days per week Mon \- Friday 8am\-16\.30pm on site * Act as system maintenance expert (SME) for Onsite Electrical systems plant \& equipment. * Be proficient with onsite asset management system (REALM). * Responsible for correct operation of equipment and on time PPM maintenance tasks within the normal days and rotating shift teams. * Take ownership of spare parts stock and sourcing replacement parts for electrical \& mechanical tasks and repairs within the site team. * Oversee Vendor service and repair visits as point of contact and ensuring tasks are completed to CBRE standards. Review reports and highlight any issues for escalation and scheduled repairs. * Ensure that comprehensive maintenance records are kept in the form of “Frontline\\" task sheets and update Log books associated with Electrical / Mechanical \& HVAC systems. * Ensure that Method Statements and Risk Assessments are prepared for all tasks carried out to ensure safe working practices. * To develop a good working relationship with all members of CBRE Managed Services staff and clients staff. * Hands on service and repairs works with a view to mentoring junior technicians and non\-technical staff. * Work closely with Facilities team in dealing with Client requests / work orders for timely close out and resolution of issues relating to Electrical / Mechanical \& HVAC systems. * Ensure a professional image of CBRE Managed Services is presented to clients and visitors, and ensure excellence in customer service is delivered and promoted at all times. * A flexible approach is required for the consistent and high standard of service CBRE provide. * At times technical upskill will be required for on\-site equipment such as BMS, fire alarm lighting controls and various equipment operational systems and devices. Accountabilities * Accountable to Contract Engineering Manager. * Operate as system champion for assigned responsible equipment within your scope and understanding. * The post holder does not have any directly reporting staff. * This post carries no direct budgetary responsibility **Core Competencies** **Education** * Trade Qualified Electrician , must have good written and spoken English and basic P.C skills. **Training** * Formally trained in Electrical installation and maintenance systems. * M.V \& L.V switching and operational understanding. Electrical AP appointed CBRE standard. * M.V \& L.V Training \& certification required. ( training can be provided for the right candidate). * Operational experience of building management control systems. ( required ). * Electrical testing, fixed wire, appliance and PAT. ( distinct advantage ). * Formal health and safety understanding \- training (Desirable) **Experience** * Proficient with electrical installation and maintenance standards. * Vendor and Contractor management experience, also team lead experience desirable. * Experience of maintaining Pumps, Motors AHU’s FCU’s heating and cooling systems. * Experience of generator \& UPS systems for 100% uptime available of comms rooms. * Critical utilities, Electrical, A/C and dual power supplies will be a distinct advantage. * Experience of operating and maintenance CMMS \& BMS, fire alarm and lighting system. * Sound understanding and experience for meeting health and safety obligations with regard to maintenance activities. (Desirable) * Working knowledge of statutory and regulatory equipment and testing requirements. * Sound understanding of HVAC and building services systems. * Some project management experience or team lead experience desirable.

title: Engineering Apprentice (Polymer)
description: **Description** At West, we’re a dedicated team that is connected by a purpose to improve patient lives that has been at the center of our Company for more than a century. Our story began when Herman O. West solved the problem of supplying penicillin in mass quantities to the US Government during World War 2\. Through our work to deliver thousands of life\-saving and life\-enhancing injectable medicines to millions of patients daily, West’s indelible mark on the healthcare industry has just begun. A name started our story. How will yours help write our future? There’s no better place to join an inclusive community of professionals with opportunities for lifelong learning, growth and development. Supported by benefit programs, we empower the physical, mental, emotional and financial health of our team members and their families. We believe in giving back to help those in need in the communities where we live and work. And are equally committed to creating a healthier environment and planet through our sustainability efforts. **Job Summary** The Polymer Apprentice will be responsible for the efficient set up and operation of polymer processing lines in the fields of injection moulding for the production of plastic components relevant to industry standards. Responsible for operating moulding and any auxiliary equipment. Responsible for inspecting product to a pre\-determined specification and completing all documentation necessary to provide quality, production and traceability records in accordance with ISO9002/EN46002/ISO14001 standards/ customer specifications. Responsible for making technical adjustments to the machine as required. Reports to the Moulding Supervisor. **Essential Duties And Responsibilities** * Take part in phased college semesters in accordance with course requirements and ensure course work and exams are completed and passed. * Ensures the quality of the parts produced by inspecting parts visually and through measuring devices. * Supporting the shift and Shift Supervisor by reporting on/resolving process/technical issues. * Ensure the machine settings as per the validated status are recorded on relevant documentation if applicable. * Attend production meetings as required. * Attend to all incidents of downtime and record in the relevant documentation on a daily basis. * Define machine status on OEE System in order to determine machine downtime and run time. * Assisting with the introduction and validation of new tooling as required. * Work closely with maintenance, tool room and process engineers to ensure technical support for the room. * Completes Tool changes. * Provide assistance, support, and training to operators on the shift. * Performs line clearance in conjunction with the relevant procedures. * Maintaining accuracy of batch documentation at all times and ensuring that it is filled in and right first time. * Ensure that machines are stopped and started as per the relevant start up procedures in manufacturing manual. * Understands and safely operates all equipment. * Reports and/or corrects unsafe working conditions. * Ensures all moulding machines and ancillary equipment are working correctly and reports any problems. * Performs mold cleaning and cleaning of machines/robots and ancillary equipment on a daily/monthly basis. * Ensures that the correct raw materials are being used at each moulding machine; ensures raw material is staged/ready for next shift. * Maintains work areas in a clean and orderly manner by practicing good housekeeping. * Maintains and updates assigned measures of performance, in conjunction with the Production Group leader. * Compliance to all site Environmental, Health and Safety requirements training, and regulations. * Compliance to all local site company policies, procedures and corporate policies. * Act in accordance with the company’s guiding principles and adherence to the corporate Code of Conduct. * Develops and maintains methods/procedures and all supporting documentation to meet the requirements of ISO 9001/ ISO 13485 / ISO 14001\. * Performs additional duties at the request of the direct supervisor. ****Education**** * Leaving Certificate or equivalent, Grade 06 at ordinary level in 5 subjects, 2 of which must be math’s and a language (English or Irish) **Basic Qualifications** * Willing to travel and temporary relocation to complete educational modules. * Flexibility to adapt to required working pattern which will include days and shift. * Must have good verbal reasoning, numerate ability and mechanical aptitude skills. * Must have good communication skills, both written and oral. * Must be willing to work on a shift basis in the business. **Preferred Knowledge, Skills And Abilities** * Previous experience working in a team environment. * Ability to work on their own initiative. * Previous production work experience in an injection\-moulding environment would be an advantage. ****Travel Requirements**** 10%: Up to 26 business days per year ****Physical Requirements**** Medium\-Exerting up to 50lb/22kg of force occasionally and/or up to 20lbs/9kg of force frequently, and/or up to 10lbs/4kgs of force constantly to move objects. **Mental Requirements** Must be able to work in a fast\-paced environment. Must be able to organize and prioritize tasks; must be detail orientated and self\-motivated. Must be able to deal with all levels of employees and work across various departments. Must be able to work independently on own projects while working concurrently with all departments. Establishes relationships with the customers and Equipment/ Raw Material suppliers in pursuit of continuous improvement and on\-going business development. West is an equal opportunity employer and we value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. If you have a special need that requires accommodation in order to apply to West, please send an email to Apply.Accommodation@westpharma.com . Where permitted by law, an offer of employment with West Pharmaceutical Services, or any of its subsidiary or affiliate companies, is contingent upon the satisfactory completion of background screening.

title: Entry Level Sales €30k-€60k salary + commission (Fast Track Opportunity)
description: **About Tangent** Tangent was created to make job applications fairer and more human. We connect you with industry expert mentors from leading companies who volunteer their time to guide you through every step of your job search. Our mentors help you improve your CV, prepare for interviews, and navigate salary negotiations with confidence. In today’s market, having a mentor can be the difference between being overlooked and getting hired. Tangent is a free social impact platform that helps talented people from all backgrounds unlock new opportunities and build meaningful careers. **Fast Track Your Application** To give you the best chance of success, we created **Fast Track by Tangent** , a guided application process and free career accelerator offered to candidates we identify as showing strong potential. It’s designed to help you stand out, prepare effectively, and move confidently through the hiring process for this role. **Through Fast Track, you’ll gain access to:** 💬 1:1 guidance from mentors already working in the industry 🧭 Insider insight into how top tech companies hire and what they look for in candidates 📈 Tailored support to refine your personal brand and communication skills 🚀 Visibility to employers building diverse, high\-performing sales teams 🤝 Opportunities to expand your professional network and gain real\-world advice from experienced mentors Fast Track helps you strengthen your application, build confidence before interviews, and connect directly with the companies shaping the future of tech. **The Company** In London and Dublin we’ve partnered with a **fast\-growing consumer tech company** that’s redefining how people connect with digital products. The company is on an exciting growth journey, backed by a talented team and innovative technology that’s changing how people interact online. They’re now expanding their **Go\-To\-Market team** and are looking for ambitious individuals who are eager to learn, grow, and make a real impact in a fast\-paced environment. **The Role** As a **Business Development Representative (BDR)** , you’ll play a key role in driving the company’s growth by identifying new opportunities, building relationships, and generating leads for the wider sales team. This is the ideal entry point for anyone looking to launch a rewarding career in tech sales. **You’ll learn how to:** * Research and identify new business opportunities * Connect with decision\-makers through LinkedIn, email, and phone * Communicate the value of cutting\-edge tech products to customers * Collaborate with account executives to set up product demos and meetings * Develop commercial awareness and resilience in a fast\-paced startup environment **Who We’re Looking For** You don’t need previous experience or a degree, just: * A passion for technology and curiosity about how companies grow * Great communication skills and enthusiasm for speaking to people * A positive, resilient attitude and eagerness to learn from feedback * A desire to launch a long\-term career in sales or business development **How to Apply** 1️⃣ Sign up on Tangent and create your profile (eligible candidates will be invited to join the Fast Track programme) 2️⃣ Share your motivation for the role so the hiring company can learn more about you 3️⃣ Your profile will be reviewed by the hiring team, and if selected, you’ll be moved forward quickly through the interview process with additional mentoring and support

title: FP&A Analyst
description: **Your role** The FP\&A Analyst role is integral to the FP\&A function as owners of the budgeting, forecasting and strategic planning models of the organisation. Reporting to the Financial Planning \& Analysis Manager, the successful candidate will support and work closely with CFO and Directors within the Finance department, as well as other senior finance team members and will focus on supporting a range of weekly, monthly, quarterly, and annual planning and reporting processes. The successful candidate will also support projects relating to various Finance and Finance system improvement processes, provide insightful and effective financial analysis for a range of Corporate and Finance functional requirements on an on\-going basis, and support other Finance projects as required. Such financial analysis will have varying degrees of complexity, urgency, and system dependency. Your responsibilities as a Financial Planning and Analysis Analyst will include: * Building and maintaining integrated three\-statement financial models across the P\&L, balance sheet and cashflow to support budgeting, forecasting, long\-term planning, scenario analysis and decision support. * Forecasting and tracking cashflow performance, including monitoring key working capital movements, liquidity drivers and variances against budget, forecast and prior periods. * Partnering with the Network team to understand key movements in schedule and route assumptions and generate statistic packs for distribution and upload to the planning system at each forecast. * Providing in depth causal analysis as part of the financial planning processes ensuring alignment with current commercial and operational activity, transformation programmes etc. * Taking ownership of outputs and reports (including excel submissions and presentations to Aer Lingus senior management and IAG Group), ensuring that submissions are made in line with deadlines, and that requests for further follow up analysis and insights are provided on a timely basis. * Supporting development and continuous improvement of planning models, reporting processes and system\-based solutions to significantly reduce manual effort and improve insight. * The FP\&A Analyst will support the following processes as required: * Quarterly forecasts. * Annual Budget. * 3 Year and long\-term planning processes. * Financial scenario modelling (including income statement, cash flow and balance sheet). * IAG Group reporting packs. * Aer Lingus Management Committee reporting packs. * Aer Lingus Board reporting packs. * Presentations to Aer Lingus Executive. * Finance Projects. * Ad Hoc reporting and accounting duties. **Your Qualifications And Key Criteria** * Qualified accountant, e.g. ACA, ACCA or CIMA or equivalent with at least 1 year’s post qualification experience in industry or professional practice in a similar role. * Excellent knowledge of MS Office, advanced Excel and PowerPoint, and experience using Microsoft Copilot and other AI tools, with the ability to develop insightful presentations within tight timeframes. * Proven ability to systematically gather, test, review, monitor, analyse and report on financial information to a high standard. * Ability to become familiar with new systems and relatively large and complex data sets on a timely independent basis. * Demonstrate strong communication, teamwork and partnership skills with a collaborative approach. * Proven interpersonal skills with the ability to build and maintain positive working relationships. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills, to include proven report writing and presentation skills. * Experience of presenting financial information to senior management and to non\-finance management. * Focused attitude to work, with the ability to consistently meet deadlines while ensuring excellent attention to detail and high levels of accuracy. * Strong commercial focus, combined with drive and determination. * Fluent in English, both written and spoken. * Eligible to live and work in the EU. * Current unrestricted worldwide passport. * Be in possession or be able to pass a strict airport security vetting procedure which includes a 5 year background check\* (or be in possession of a current airside pass for the airport this position is based in Dublin). Closing Date: 29th May 2026\. At Aer Lingus, we embrace diversity and believe in providing equal opportunities to all qualified individuals. We are dedicated to cultivating an inclusive and respectful work environment that values employees' unique backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We do not discriminate based on race, gender, sexual orientation, age, disability, or any other protected status. If you have any questions or require any accommodations to support you in your application, please contact us at recruit@aerlingus.com. **Division / Department** Finance \- Finance, Tax \& Treasury

title: FP&A Analyst (Hedge Fund)
description: **About the job** A leading global investment manager is building out its Finance team and this role sits right at the center of it. This is not a back\-office reporting role. It’s a commercial, forward\-looking FP\&A seat with real visibility to senior leadership and direct involvement in decision making. **The Role** * Own monthly financial dashboards for senior management. * Analyze revenue, cost drivers and KPIs to improve forecasting accuracy. * Partner with business leaders on budgeting and strategic initiatives. * Build financial models to support product expansion and growth decisions. * Work cross\-functionally with Risk, Strategy and HR on firm wide projects. * Support financial reporting as required. **The Opportunity** * Newly created team in Dublin \- genuine opportunity to shape and influence. * Direct exposure to global FP\&A teams. * Strong blend of commercial FP\&A \+ technical finance / reporting. * High visibility role with interaction at senior stakeholder level. * Fast\-paced, high\-performance environment where strong analysts thrive. **Requirements** * 4\+ years FP\&A experience (financial services preferred). * Strong Excel (VBA / macros a plus) and analytical skillset. * Ability to communicate clearly and influence stakeholders. * Comfortable operating in a fast\-moving, high\-pressure environment.

title: Fabricator
description: Cental is a global supplier of fully designed, engineered, built and integrated modular and skid\-mounted solutions for the Data Centre, Utilities, Life Sciences, and Oil \& Gas markets. From our headquarters in Carlow, Ireland, we deliver comprehensive solutions for clients worldwide. Our people are the foundation of our success. With three manufacturing facilities and a talented team of engineers, designers, project managers, and tradespeople, our team manages every detail, ensuring seamless delivery from initial design to final installation. We value teamwork, respect, and continuous improvement, creating a collaborative, innovative environment where everyone can reach their potential. We also believe in celebrating achievements and recognising effort. From monthly quality and safety initiatives for breakfast treats and local tool vouchers, to company breakfasts, staff BBQs, and occasional outings, we bring people together to enjoy achievements and strengthen our team spirit. Growth and learning are central to Cental. Whether you’re joining as an apprentice, graduate, or experienced professional, we provide training and development opportunities to help you build a rewarding, long\-term career. If you’re passionate about excellence, innovation, and teamwork, join us and be part of a company that’s engineering solutions and building futures. Cental is seeking a Fabricator who will be responsible for cutting, shaping, assembling, and welding metal components to produce high‑quality steel structures, frames, and modular components in line with engineering drawings, quality standards, and safety requirements. The role supports high‑volume manufacturing in a fast‑paced environment, ensuring accuracy, efficiency, and consistent product quality. **Key Responsibilities** * Interpret and work from technical drawings, blueprints, and fabrication plans. * Fabricate steel components using cutting, grinding, drilling, forming, and welding processes. * Assemble structural components ensuring accurate dimensions and tolerances. * Operate fabrication equipment such as: + MIG/Stick welding machines + Plasma cutters + Saws (band saw, chop saw) + Press brakes and rollers + Grinders and power tools * Conduct weld preparation, finishing, and quality checks. * Ensure welds meet internal quality standards and any relevant codes. * Inspect finished products for defects or deviations. * Complete rework where required. * Ensure compliance with quality procedures, ISO standards, and project specifications. * Adhere strictly to company safety policies, including PPE requirements. * Work collaboratively with engineering, production, and assembly teams. **Skills \& Experience** * Proven experience as a Fabricator in a manufacturing or engineering environment. * Strong ability to read and interpret fabrication and engineering drawings. * Skilled in MIG welding. * Competent in measuring, marking out, and working to tight tolerances. * Experience using fabrication workshop tools and machinery. * Strong attention to detail and commitment to quality. * Ability to work independently and as part of a team.

title: Field Sales Executive
description: **About Us** Nuvo Charge is a rapidly growing technology company delivering innovative mobile charging solutions across high\-footfall venues throughout Ireland. Our charging stations are designed to meet the needs of modern consumers who rely heavily on mobile devices while on the move. As we expand nationwide, we are building a high\-performance field sales team to drive rapid adoption across hospitality, leisure, healthcare, and public venues. Our growth requires ambitious, commercially driven individuals who thrive in fast\-paced, target\-focused environments and are motivated by autonomy, earning potential, and impact. **The Role** The Field Sales Executive is responsible for driving the expansion of the Nuvo Charge network across Ireland. The role focuses on identifying, engaging, and securing new venue partners for the installation of charging units in high dwell\-time environments. This is a national field\-based role requiring a proactive and self\-sufficient sales professional who can generate leads, manage a pipeline, close partnerships, and ensure successful installation and onboarding of new locations. The role is central to the continued growth of the Nuvo Charge network and revenue expansion. **What You'll Do** * Identify, prospect, and secure new venue partnerships across Ireland * Conduct in\-person meetings with venue owners and decision\-makers to present and close opportunities * Deliver clear and compelling product demonstrations and value propositions * Generate your own leads and build a strong pipeline of opportunities * Follow up consistently on all prospects to drive deal progression * Negotiate and close commercial agreements with venue partners * Coordinate and support successful installation of Nuvo Charge units * Ensure smooth onboarding of new venue locations * Achieve and exceed monthly installation and sales targets * Maintain accurate CRM records and pipeline reporting * Provide feedback and insights from the field to support commercial strategy * Represent the Nuvo Charge brand professionally at all times **What We're Looking For** * Proven experience in field sales or direct business development * Strong communication, negotiation, and closing skills * Highly self\-motivated, target\-driven, and results\-focused * Ability to work independently across a national territory * Full, clean driving licence * Strong organisational skills and disciplined follow\-up approach * Comfortable working in a fast\-paced, high\-growth environment **Nice to Have** * Experience in hospitality, venue\-based, or B2B field sales * Understanding of partnership\-based or recurring revenue sales models * Familiarity with CRM systems and structured sales pipelines * Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to build relationships quickly * Interest in technology\-enabled consumer solutions or retail tech

title: Field Service Engineer
description: **Who We Are** Applied Materials is a global leader in materials engineering solutions used to produce virtually every new chip and advanced display in the world. We design, build and service cutting\-edge equipment that helps our customers manufacture display and semiconductor chips – the brains of devices we use every day. As the foundation of the global electronics industry, Applied enables the exciting technologies that literally connect our world – like AI and IoT. If you want to push the boundaries of materials science and engineering to create next generation technology, join us to deliver material innovation that changes the world. **What We Offer** Location: Leixlip,IRL You’ll benefit from a supportive work culture that encourages you to learn, develop, and grow your career as you take on challenges and drive innovative solutions for our customers. We empower our team to push the boundaries of what is possible—while learning every day in a supportive leading global company. Visit our Careers website to learn more. At Applied Materials, we care about the health and wellbeing of our employees. We’re committed to providing programs and support that encourage personal and professional growth and care for you at work, at home, or wherever you may go. Learn more about our benefits. As a Field Service Engineer \[Customer Engineer] at Applied Materials, you serve as the direct liaison with customers, collaborating closely to install, maintain, and upgrade equipment. You'll use digital analytics for troubleshooting and apply basic diagnostic techniques to assess and address technical issues. Your responsibilities include performing preventative and corrective maintenance on various systems such as electrical, vacuum, mechanical, plasma, hydraulic, and gas systems. Additionally, you'll coordinate and communicate directly with customers to ensure smooth operations and exceptional service. **What You’ll Do** As a Field Service Engineer, you'll serve as Applied’s direct liaison with our customers and quickly solve high\-value problems on their behalf. By collaborating closely with our customers onsite to install, maintain, and upgrade Applied Materials equipment, and with our internal teams to improve business processes, you’ll make a significant impact on ensuring peak performance and deliver exceptional service, while becoming an integral part of Applied Materials' diverse and dynamic field team. ***Applicants must be flexible to work a 24/7 shift pattern and willing to travel off\-site for up to 10 weeks for training*** **Role Responsibilities:** * Perform field\-based installation, configuration, maintenance, and upgrades of complex Applied Materials equipment, ensuring compliance with technical specifications and operational standards * Use digital analytics for troubleshooting * Apply basic diagnostic techniques to assess equipment and address technical issues * Perform preventative and corrective maintenance on electrical, vacuum, mechanical, plasma, hydraulic, and gas systems (training provided) * Coordinate and communicate directly with customers * Fulfil additional duties, as assigned **Minimum Qualifications:** * Successful completion of an Associate Degree (or higher) in a relevant field. * At least 2 years of work experience in customer/field service support, or a related technical field * Ability to read and interpret electrical and mechanical schematics * Experience diagnosing and resolving basic technical challenges * Basic mechanical aptitude with pneumatics, hydraulics, electronics, vacuum, or thermodynamics * Familiarity with hand tools and their appropriate usage, including digital multimeters * Effective written and verbal communication skills * Basic knowledge of Microsoft Excel, Word, and PowerPoint * Able to meet all on\-site requirements, including safety and environmental guidelines **Physical Requirements:** This position often requires extensive walking, standing for extended periods of time, working in tight spaces, bending, squatting, twisting, kneeling, reaching, climbing stairs and ladders, working at height, lifting up to 35 lbs., the ability to distinguish between colors and working with electricity, the ability to work in an environment with noise that may be difficult for some individuals with sensitivity to noise, and the use of hand and power tools. Additionally, because this position generally involves working in a clean room, it requires the use of appropriate Personal Protective Equipment such as coveralls, hoods, booties, safety glasses, gloves, respirators, chemical aprons, and face shields. **Additional Information** **Time Type:** Full time **Employee Type:** Assignee / Regular **Travel:** Yes, 20% of the Time **Relocation Eligible:** No Applied Materials is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, national origin, citizenship, ancestry, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, veteran or military status, or any other basis prohibited by law.

title: Field Technician & Operations Associate
description: **About Us** Speed Queen Self Service Laundry is part of **DNL Collective** , a growing group focused on building and scaling operationally strong, multi\-site businesses across Ireland and the UK. Our laundry division is one of the fastest\-growing self\-service operators in Europe, with 40\+ locations and continued expansion underway into the UK. We build and operate high\-quality launderette locations with a focus on reliability, efficiency, and customer experience. As we scale, we’re looking for ambitious individuals who want to grow with the business and take on real responsibility early. **The Role** This is a hands\-on, fast\-paced role combining **field\-based technical work with operational and project support** .
