title: HR Analyst (Graduate)
description: **HR Analyst (Graduate)** **Duration: 6 months** **Location: Dublin 4** **Role Overview:** The HR Analyst, reporting to the HR Business Partner, supports the administrative, analytical and operational HR activities within a regulated financial services environment. Working as part of a small collaborative team, this role offers broad exposure to the full employee life cycle with an emphasis on data accuracy, HR systems, reporting, and continuous process improvement. This is a varied role that involves regular interaction with stakeholders at all levels of the organisation, contribution to HR initiatives and projects, and the use of HR data and technology to support evidence‑based decision‑making. The role provides an excellent opportunity to develop capability in HR operations, analytics, and systems within a regulated environment. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys combining HR operations with data, systems, and continuous improvement, and who is motivated by making HR processes more effective through insight and technology. **About the team:** The successful candidate will join a professional HR team of three, consisting of the Head of HR, HR Business Partner, and Senior HR Associate. The team is based on‑site four days a week and works closely with the business to deliver high‑quality HR support to the Dublin workforce, aligned with our organisational values. The team has full ownership of HR activities locally and are supported by the wider HR network within our parent organisation, providing access to additional expertise, guidance, and opportunities for collaboration. As a team, we support one another, are curious, take initiative, act with accountability, and actively look for ways to improve HR processes and our own professional development. **Main Responsibilities:** **HR Administration** * Manage end‑to‑end administration of HR processes across the employee life cycle, ensuring accurate, timely, and well‑structured data is maintained at all stages. * Maintain and continuously improve HR records within HRIS and local filing systems, ensuring data integrity, GDPR compliance, and alignment with internal policies. * Actively monitor data quality within HR systems, identifying discrepancies, trends, or inefficiencies and proposing practical solutions. * Manage centralised HR inboxes, responding to queries efficiently and using recurring themes to identify opportunities for process improvement or automation. **Recruitment Support** * Coordinate recruitment activities end\-to\-end, including posting vacancies, liaising with hiring managers and recruitment agencies, managing candidate applications, scheduling interviews, participating in interview panels and contributing to selection decisions. * Leverage and maintain recruitment data to support efficient, compliant hiring processes. * Maintain recruitment records, ensuring GDPR compliance. * Support the analysis of recruitment metrics and contribute insights to improve hiring outcomes. * Partner with hiring managers and the HR team to enhance recruitment reporting and identify opportunities to streamline processes or improve the candidate journey. **Payroll \& Benefits** * Assist with the preparation of monthly payroll, ensuring accurate and timely delivery. * Act as the point of contact for staff benefits, maintain the local benefits SharePoint site ensuring information is to up to date and accurate. * Liaise with benefit providers to ensure smooth administration, communicate benefits updates and initiatives to staff, track and monitor benefits and leave usage. **Compliance and Regulatory Support** * Support the Fitness \& Probity process \- ensuring regulatory checks, approvals, declarations and ongoing compliance obligations are administered and monitored. * Assist with internal and external HR audits. **Reporting, Analytics \& Process Automation** * Prepare HR reports, dashboards and metrics for management, analysing HR data to provide actionable insights on trends, supporting evidence\-based decision making. * Partner with the Head of HR and HR Business Partner during committee cycles, preparing papers, reports, and responding to information requests as required. * Identify manual, repetitive, or inefficient HR processes and contribute to the design and implementation of automated or technology‑enabled solutions. * Utilise HR analytical systems and tools to extract accurate data to support informed decision\-making across the team and wider business. * Work collaboratively with the HR team and wider HR network to identify opportunities for continuous improvement across HR operations. **Skills and Experience:** We’re looking for someone who brings relevant experience, curiosity, and a collaborative approach to HR work. You don’t need to meet every requirement to apply — if this role interests you, we’d welcome your application. * Clear and respectful communication skills, with the ability to work effectively with colleagues and stakeholders at different levels of the organisation. * Ability to organise and prioritise work in a changing environment, while maintaining attention to detail. * A collaborative approach, with a willingness to take ownership of tasks, share knowledge, and support the team objectives. * A high level of professionalism, discretion, and care when working with confidential or sensitive information. * Experience working in an HR administration, operations or analytics role, ideally within a regulated, financial services, or professional services environment. * Familiar with using HR systems or databases to maintain records and produce accurate information. * Ability to prepare HR reports or metrics and interpret data to identify trends and support informed decision‑making. * Confident working with Microsoft Office suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) for analysis and reporting. * An interest in HR technology, process improvement, and automation, with a practical and solution‑focused mindset. * A relevant qualification in Human Resources, Business, Analytics or a related area, or equivalent practical experience. We are committed to building a diverse, inclusive, and supportive workplace where everyone feels valued and able to do their best work. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities, and experiences. If you require any reasonable adjustments or accommodations at any stage of the recruitment process, please let us know — we’re happy to support you. *All data received from applicants is subject to GDPR and Data Protection requirements.*

title: Human Resources Graduate
description: **HR Graduate \- People Department** **Our Story:** The Keogh family have been farming the fertile lands of North County Dublin for over 200 years. Fast forward to today and our family has grown to include more than just flesh and blood; we’ve got an amazing team at the farm, all of whom we consider as family none\-the\-less. We love working with committed and inspiring people to create something our grandparents would be proud of. From our proud family heritage, hard work and passion for quality, we at Keogh’s Farm are committed to producing Ireland’s finest potatoes, crisps, and snacks that are truly… Grown with Love in Ireland. **Purpose of Role:** This is an exciting opportunity for a motivated graduate with internship experience to join our dynamic People team. The role offers hands‑on exposure and real responsibility within a fast‑paced, challenging, and rewarding environment where learning and development are strongly encouraged. As a valued member of the People team, you will play a key role in supporting the delivery of our People initiatives across **HR Processes and Procedures, Values and Culture, Talent Acquisition, and Learning and Development** . This role provides an excellent platform to build practical HR experience, develop your skill set, and grow your career within a supportive and collaborative organisation. **Key Duties and Responsibilities:** * Support the **Talent Acquisition process** , including candidate screening, interview coordination, and managing recruitment correspondence in line with business needs. * Coordinate and support **Employee Induction Programmes** , ensuring new starters have a positive onboarding experience. * Maintain and update **Time Management and HR systems** , ensuring employee records are accurate, compliant, and up to date. * Provide administrative support to the **Performance Management** and **Career Path Development Programmes** , helping to promote continuous development across the business. * Research, organise, track, and report on **training and development activities** , supporting employee growth. * Compile and generate **weekly, monthly, and quarterly HR reports** to support data‑driven decision‑making. * Deliver general **HR administrative support** , including filing, scanning, and managing internal correspondence. * Organise HR‑related meetings, including **agenda preparation, note** ‑ **taking, and distribution of minutes** . * Act as a first point of support for employees by assisting the People team with **HR queries and resolutions** . * Support the **implementation and ongoing maintenance of HR policies and procedures** , ensuring clear communication and consistency. * Coordinate the **People \& Values Calendar** and contribute to various **wellbeing initiatives** across the organisation. * Provide flexible support across HR operations, taking on **additional administrative duties** as business needs evolve. **The Ideal Candidate will possess the following knowledge and experience:** * A **third** ‑ **level qualification in Human Resources** or a related discipline. * **6 months to 1 year of experience** in a similar role or relevant internship is desirable. * Strong proficiency in **Microsoft Office applications** . * Excellent **interpersonal and communication skills** , with the ability to engage effectively at all levels of the organisation. * Well‑developed **organisational skills** , with the ability to manage priorities and meet deadlines. * Strong **analytical abilities** with a high standard of **report writing** . * Clear and effective **verbal and written communication skills** . * A **creative, innovative, and proactive approach** to tasks and initiatives. Please email your CV and cover letter to peopleteam@keoghs.ie ***Keogh’s Crisps is an Equal Opportunities Employer***

title: IT Asset Specialist
description: **About The Role** We are seeking a detail\-oriented and highly organized **IT Asset Specialist** to join our growing Data Center Operations team. This full\-time role supports inventory control and asset lifecycle management across multiple data center locations. As an IT Asset Specialist, you will serve as the local authority for all on\-site inventory, and an owner of systems, and reporting improvements— ensuring accuracy, compliance, and operational efficiency while helping expand best practices. Location: Fully on\-site in either Allen or Fort Worth, TX Salary: Between $70k and $80k, and includes industry\-leading benefits, perks, and equity. **What You’ll Do** **Asset Management** * Track all on\-site assets, including inbound and outbound shipments; maintain accurate records within inventory systems. * Maintain accurate records within inventory systems. Contribute to systems, tooling, reporting insights or improve processes * Document full asset lifecycle (procurement → deployment → disposal) and own data integrity for asset records and system usage. * Conduct regular audits across multiple tracking systems; identify and implement process and reporting improvements. * Solve ambiguous/non\-routine problems independently, escalating when necessary; drive root\-cause fixes and follow\-up actions. * Oversee maintenance, upgrades, decommissioning, and disposal with procedural compliance. * Create and maintain documentation for asset processes and procedures **Parts \& Inventory Administration** * Manage parts inventory (scan items in/out of parts lockers), perform routine audits, and run inventory usage reports. * Collaborate with internal teams to maintain appropriate stock levels * Coordinate RMAs with vendors and manage escalations to resolution. * Act as go\-to expert/mentor for Asset related issues at location **Shipping \& Receiving** * Prepare sites for incoming shipments, inspect inbound packages, and log into inventory systems with accuracy. * Package and coordinate outbound shipments with delivery partners; maintain detailed shipping records and ensure successful deliveries. * Process and securely store incoming hardware with ownership of chain\-of\-custody documentation. **Access \& Vendor Coordination** * Manage physical and system access via internal data center portals * Approve vendor and third\-party access requests * Coordinate with on\-site vendors to ensure project completion * Handle RMA escalations and communicate with Operations teams **Collaboration** * Partner closely with the IT Asset Manager, Data Center Technicians, and Site Manager to align on company\-wide asset standards. * Ensure alignment with company\-wide asset management standards and processes **Required Qualifications** **What We’re Looking For** * Exceptional organizational skills and strong attention to detail * Experience using spreadsheets (Excel or Google Sheets) and familiarity with database systems. * Experience with ticketing systems and comfortable resolving ambiguous issues independently. * Basic computer component knowledge and strong communication skills in a technical environment. * Demonstrated ownership mindset for data integrity, system usage, and process. **Nice to Have** * Exposure to technical environments (IT, data centers) * Basic understanding of IT hardware and infrastructure * Familiarity with Jira ticketing system * Familiarity with Asset Panda **Work Environment** * Full\-time, on\-site role (Monday–Friday) Day shift * Assigned to a specific data center location * Reports directly to an IT Asset Manager with cross\-functional collaboration on\-site *Voltage Park is an equal opportunity employer and makes employment decisions on the basis of merit. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic under federal, state, or local law. If you require an accommodation during the job application process, please notify your recruiter.*

title: IT Internal Auditor
description: Because our tomorrow is in your hands. Finance, our way. Finance at Primark Our finance team is renowned for their attention to detail. You’ll enable us to navigate the financial landscape with precision, contributing to the strategic decisions that support our growth. Surround yourself with experts and bring your scientific mind to shape the future of our organisation. **What You’ll Do** In your role, you’ll collaborate with different people across a range of skillsets. Here’s a flavour of your day\-to\-day: * Partner with stakeholders across the business to define the IT risk universe and shape a robust, risk\-based IT audit plan * Lead and continuously evolve the IT audit approach, driving best practice and consistency across the organisation * Deliver a wide range of IT audit and risk engagements, working closely with Internal Audit colleagues and third\-party providers, and embedding data analytics into audit activity * Design and execute IT audit programmes that assess key business, operational and regulatory risks * Provide IT risk advice and subject\-matter expertise to support projects, system implementations and change initiatives * Lead IT investigations and post\-incident reviews, collaborating with the wider business to strengthen controls and improve risk management quality **What You’ll Get** The work you do shapes the impact we have. We’re focused on providing you with the culture to reach your potential. Your wellbeing is our top priority. Let’s talk lifestyle: Healthcare, pension, and potential bonus. 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Bring** We want every person in our organisation to feel like they’re making a key contribution. Here are some of the experiences and skills you’ll need for the role: * At least 3 years’ experience in IT audit, IT assurance, IT risk or IT security, supported by a third\-level qualification in a related discipline (professional certifications such as CISA, CISM, CISSP, ITIL or accountancy qualifications are desirable but not essential) * Excellent attention to detail, time management and organisational skills, with the ability to prioritise, multitask and consistently meet deadlines * Strong analytical capability, including the ability to interrogate large volumes of data while maintaining a clear, high\-level risk perspective * Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, with confidence presenting complex information clearly to diverse business audiences * A collaborative, professional approach with proven ability to build relationships, influence outcomes, coach others and manage sensitive, confidential matters with integrity * Sound judgement, commercial awareness and a value‑add mindset, demonstrating high ethical standards, diplomacy and accountability in everything you do Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business a over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. **REQ ID:** JR\-7947

title: Incident Response Team Analyst (Law Enforcement-Physical Security), EMEA
description: This is a short\-term employee (STE) position with an anticipated duration of \[15] months. STEs are Meta employees who receive competitive base compensation and benefits. Please note that this role does not include equity compensation and has a fixed end date. There is no guarantee of conversion to a full\-time role; any full\-time opportunity would require a separate application and interview process.The Meta legal team is seeking a candidate with experience managing complex workflows, maintaining accurate documentation, and meeting deadlines in operational environments with experience reviewing and documenting information to join our Incident Response Team. The right candidate must have a track record of delivering results in environments requiring timely response to escalations with defined service\-level expectations, demonstrated ability to make well\-reasoned decisions in ambiguous or high\-stakes situations, and experience collaborating with cross\-functional teams and external stakeholders. Incident Response Team Analyst (Law Enforcement\-Physical Security), EMEA Responsibilities: * Bring operational depth to a team that evaluates threat, risk and user privacy in an environment centered around time\-critical emergency escalations * Review and assess inbound emergency escalations * make immediate decisions based on a variety of complex factors that will include imminence, sensitive issues and graphic content and coordinate with a number of internal and external partners including law enforcement in an attempt to ensure user’s safety and mitigate real\-world harm * Convey clear and concise responses to questions from law enforcement, government agencies, and internal teams regarding Incident Response policy and process * Work with cross\-functional teams to drive improvements to policies and processes across teams * Available for weekend work on a rotational basis regularly * This role may involve exposure to potentially graphic and/or objectionable content including but not limited to graphic images, videos, audio and posts, offensive or derogatory language, and other potentially objectionable material, i.e. child exploitation, graphic violence, self\-injury, and other content which may be considered offensive or disturbing Minimum Qualifications: * Proven ability to make important decisions independently and prioritize competing urgent tasks based on impact, imminence, and stakeholder needs, respond quickly to changing situations in complex environments while maintaining accuracy and adherence to established protocols * Demonstrated ability to work independently and manage tasks with minimal supervision * 2\+ years of experience in analyzing information to assess imminent risk and threats to life * Ability to adapt communication style to suit different stakeholders like cross\-functional teams and law enforcement * Ability to work with potentially graphic and objectionable content * Experience with social media, analytical tools and online research Preferred Qualifications: * Experience adhering to and implementing responsible, ethical AI practices (e.g., risk assessment, bias mitigation, quality and accuracy reviews) * Demonstrated ability to integrate AI tools to optimize/redesign workflows and drive measurable impact (e.g., efficiency gains, quality improvements) * Demonstrated ongoing AI skill development (e.g., prompt/context engineering, agent orchestration) and staying current with emerging AI technologies * Proficiency in additional languages (e.g., Hindi or other languages spoken in India \[e.g. Tamil, Telugu, Bengali) is preferred because the role supports global emergency escalations where communications may be received in local languages * Experience with Crisis Management, Government Agencies or Law Enforcement * Demonstrated ongoing AI skill development (e.g., prompt/context engineering, agent orchestration) and staying current with emerging AI technologies * Direct experience in handling emergency response escalations About Meta: Meta builds technologies that help people connect, find communities, and grow businesses. When Facebook launched in 2004, it changed the way people connect. Apps like Messenger, Instagram and WhatsApp further empowered billions around the world. Now, Meta is moving beyond 2D screens toward immersive experiences like augmented and virtual reality to help build the next evolution in social technology. People who choose to build their careers by building with us at Meta help shape a future that will take us beyond what digital connection makes possible today—beyond the constraints of screens, the limits of distance, and even the rules of physics. Individual compensation is determined by skills, qualifications, experience, and location. Compensation details listed in this posting reflect the base hourly rate, monthly rate, or annual salary only, and do not include bonus, equity or sales incentives, if applicable. In addition to base compensation, Meta offers benefits. Learn more about benefits at Meta.

title: Information Technology Support Engineer
description: My Dublin based client is recruiting for an experienced IT Support Engineer to provide operational support during a critical go\-live phase and will be key in ensuring a smooth rollout, resolving issues quickly, and maintaining stable IT services as operations scale. This is a 6 month contract. **Key Responsibilities** * Deliver on\-site and remote IT support during the go\-live phase. * Monitor, maintain, and troubleshoot Windows environments, including Active Directory and Azure AD. * Manage device provisioning, patching, and software deployment using SCCM, Intune, and Windows Autopatch. * Serve as the primary point of contact for incidents and service requests, ensuring prompt resolution with minimal business impact. * Maintain up\-to\-date documentation for systems, configurations, and incident resolutions. * Work closely with internal teams and external vendors to resolve technical issues. * Support business continuity and disaster recovery processes. * Automate routine tasks using PowerShell scripting. **Skills \& Experience** * Strong hands\-on experience with Microsoft Windows, Active Directory, and Azure AD. * Solid expertise in endpoint management and patching tools, including SCCM and Intune. * Demonstrated ability to troubleshoot and resolve issues in a fast\-paced environment. * Excellent communication skills, with the ability to engage both technical and non\-technical stakeholders. * Experience with PowerShell scripting. **Qualifications** * Minimum of 5 years’ experience in IT support or operations. * Degree or diploma in IT, Computer Science, or a related discipline. * Microsoft certifications are an advantage.

title: Intern, Software Engineer
description: **Job Requisition ID \#** 25WD92001 **Position Overview** At Autodesk, we’re reimagining what’s possible. As a global leader in 3D design, engineering, and entertainment software, we help people everywhere imagine, design, and create a better world. Right now, we’re transforming how our products work by moving to the cloud—opening up exciting opportunities for collaboration, innovation, and impact. What sets us apart isn’t just our technology, but our culture. At Autodesk, you’ll find an inclusive, supportive environment where early\-career talent can grow, be creative, and make real impact. It’s one of the reasons we’ve been consistently recognized by **Fortune, Forbes, and Glassdoor as a top place to work** . Over three months in our Summer 2026 Internship Programme, you’ll gain hands\-on experience, mentorship, and opportunities to grow your engineering and domain expertise, while making real contributions to Autodesk’s manufacturing and construction technologies. Alongside technical growth, you’ll develop soft skills and build connections with other interns, locally in your office, regionally across EMEA, and globally, through programs designed just for interns. As a Software Engineer Intern on the Developer Enablement team, you’ll contribute directly to building and scaling applications that utilize environmental data, delivered through a unified standardized interface. You’ll receive mentorship, guidance, and support, while also having the autonomy and responsibility to make a real impact. **Responsibilities** * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to understand the full software development lifecycle * Contribute to real commercial development projects, guided by experienced Autodesk developers * Gain hands\-on experience with TypeScript, AWS, automated testing frameworks, DevOps, and more * Apply Agile and Scrum practices, building your project management skills * Use source control and Continuous Integration platforms such as GitHub and Jenkins * Develop robust APIs to enable sustainability analysis for Autodesk customers * Share your progress and achievements in team meetings, gaining feedback and recognition **Minimum Qualifications** * Currently pursuing a degree in a STEM field (e.g., Computer Science, Engineering) * Expected graduation date: please include your month and year of graduation in your application * Experience with API development (REST and GraphQL), through coursework, personal projects, or previous internships * Programming experience (TypeScript preferred) * Enthusiasm for learning new technologies and a proactive approach to problem\-solving **The Ideal Candidate** * Familiarity with automated testing principles to help write reliable, maintainable code * Familiarity with AWS or other cloud platforms, giving you a head start in cloud\-based development * Additional experience with other programming languages or frameworks is a plus * Strong communication skills and ability to work in a team environment * Previous internship experience in a related field is advantageous Learn More **About Autodesk** Welcome to Autodesk! Amazing things are created every day with our software – from the greenest buildings and cleanest cars to the smartest factories and biggest hit movies. We help innovators turn their ideas into reality, transforming not only how things are made, but what can be made. We take great pride in our culture here at Autodesk – it’s at the core of everything we do. Our culture guides the way we work and treat each other, informs how we connect with customers and partners, and defines how we show up in the world. When you’re an Autodesker, you can do meaningful work that helps build a better world designed and made for all. Ready to shape the world and your future? Join us! **Salary transparency** Salary is one part of Autodesk’s competitive compensation package. Offers are based on the candidate’s experience, educational level, and geographic location. **Diversity \& Belonging** We take pride in cultivating a culture of belonging where everyone can thrive. Learn more here: https://www.autodesk.com/company/diversity\-and\-belonging

title: Investment Advisor
description: At Julius Baer, we celebrate and value the individual qualities you bring, enabling you to be impactful, to be entrepreneurial, to be empowered, and to create value beyond wealth. Let’s shape the future of wealth management together. We are looking for an Investment Advisor to join the growing team in our Dublin office, responsible for developing investment proposals and providing after\-sales support, for both Discretionary and Advisory clients. The primary role of the Investment Advisor is to act as the main point of contact for clients, relationship managers and branch management for all investment\-related topics. You will act as IWMS’ ambassador inside and outside the Bank, as a primary contact for Relationship Managers (RMs) and as Subject Matters Expert (SME) for matters related to Discretionary solutions ad selected advisory mandates. You will support RMs in prospecting new clients, managing existing client portfolios and ensuring retention, by providing holistic investment and portfolio advice across all asset classes. Furthermore, you will also be participating in prospect and client meetings, guiding RMs through the bank’s investment strategy and product offering as well as hosting regular desk meetings. **YOUR CHALLENGE** * Act as representative and ambassador of Julius Baer and IWMS internally and externally * Liaise with technical specialists within the Bank in preparing and delivering investment proposals * Act as an unbiased and holistic business enabler for client solutions including cross\-selling activities (Advisory and Discretionary mandated solutions, Wealth \& Tax Planning etc.) * Ensure client service and one\-voice message on investment related topic * Ensure the sales and maintenance of Core\-Satellite selected solutions * Acquisition: Prospect pitches for discretionary and advisory solutions * Manage your own client portfolios by monitoring and analysing portfolios * Provide proactive high\-quality tailor\-made and regulatory compliant investment advice to sophisticated and active private clients, including conducting portfolio proposals / reviews, creating sophisticated, tailor\-made proposals and presenting the results to clients / prospects, by always considering client suitability, risk appetite, life\-cycle\-related needs and investment objectives * Assist RMs with market and investment product related matters * Support RMs with client interactions, both prospects as well as existing clients (i.e. join prospect/client meetings). * Support sales and after\-sales for standard DM solutions * Support internal and external events, notably + Lead / run sales and desk meetings, by providing updates on markets, sharing Julius Baer convictions on products and delivering tailored content and on demand presentations. + Presenting at internal and external events **YOUR PROFILE** * 3\+ years of relevant professional experience * Familiarity with multi\-asset investing and institutional investment strategies. * Demonstrable interest and understanding of financial markets and the main asset classes. * High level of competence in Microsoft Office products (particularly Excel, Outlook, Word, PowerPoint). * Knowledge of UCITS, MiFID II or SFDR regulatory requirements beneficial. * CFA qualification, or working towards, would be beneficial * Bachelor’s/Master’s degree in Economics/Finance/Mathematics or related discipline * Ability to prioritise own workload, exhibiting excellent organisational skills and attention to detail. * Familiarity with financial information tools (eg. Bloomberg). We are looking forward to receiving your full job application through our online application tool. Further interesting job opportunities can be found on our Career site. Is this not quite what you are looking for? Set up a **job alert** by creating a candidate account here.

title: Investment Analyst

You will likely have: * ACA qualification with strong academics from undergraduate through to professional exams * Big 4 trained with 3\+ years of experience in corporate finance, M\&A, transaction services or a related advisory role * Proven financial modelling skills with strong attention to detail * The ability to work on multiple transactions simultaneously in a fast\-paced environment * Strong communication skills and the confidence to engage with senior stakeholders This is a standout opportunity for someone looking to make the move from advisory into a principal investing seat early in their career. This role offers real deal exposure from day one, a collaborative and ambitious team environment, and a clear path to grow with the business over the long term. *For more information, apply within or contact Conor Dignam at cdignam@alliedtalent.ie*

title: Junior Epos and Stock Management Assistant
description: The Wright Group is looking for aa Epos and Stock Management Assistant to support our EPOS systems across multiple hospitality venues. title: Junior QEHS Officer
description: Quinn Downes Group are a leading National provider of Mechanical Service Installations to high\-profile clients across Ireland. We have an exciting opportunity for a **Junior** **Health \& Safety Officer** to join our expanding team. The successful candidate will join the Quinn Downes Group QEHS Department reporting to the QEHS Manager. **Main Duties and Responsibilities** * Work with and report to the QEHS Manager to identify and mitigate site\-specific hazards. * Liaise with the Contracts Manager \& Site Manager onsite to ensure compliance with current Health and Safety statutory requirements and Company procedures. * Attend regular Health \& Safety meetings between staff, subcontractors and senior management. * Conduct site inspections, risk assessments and audits. * Delivering on continual improvement on existing ISO 9001, 14001 and 45001 standards. * Participation in yearly NSAI surveillance and recertification audits. * Reviewing and assisting with the development of Risk Assessments and Method Statements (RAMS) for employee's and subcontractors. * Continual review of Health and Safety Documents, Processes and Procedures. * Detailed reporting and investigation of incidents, accidents, near misses and dangerous occurrences and updating associated databases. * Reporting, recommending, and implementing corrective actions for Health and Safety nonconformities. * Communicating good practice and good catches to relevant parties. * Trend Analysis across incidents, non\-conformances and good catches. * Conducting, organising training including Toolbox Talks. * Travel to sites within Ireland will be required **Desired Criteria** * Holds or working towards NEBOSH General Certificate or equivalent * Knowledge of Health \& Safety Legislation, Codes of Practice and Guidelines. * 1\+ years previous experience in a Health and Safety role. * Experience in the construction industry is preferred but not essential. * Full Driving license essential for travel to sites. * Must be IT Proficient with Microsoft Outlook, Excel, and Word. * Confident, proactive and have strong interpersonal and communication skills. * Willingness to uphold health and safety values above all others.

title: Junior Quality Controller
description: Job Req Number: 101473 Time Type: Full Time At DSV we're building the world’s leading logistics and transport network. With this exciting growth comes opportunity — and we’re looking for new talent to join us. **Role:** Junior Quality Controller **Location:** Dublin 11 **Hours:** Monday to Friday, 7:00 to 15:30 We are looking for a Junior Quality Controller to join our Contract Logistics operation. This role supports site\-level quality compliance through audits, document control, and continuous improvement activities. Working closely with the Site and Quality Managers, you will help ensure that operational processes meet internal standards and external certification requirements, contributing to a strong quality and safety culture across the site. **What will you be doing?** * Carry out daily quality inspections and internal audits across site operations. * Support document control activities, including Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and work instructions. * Review and question processes where product or service quality concerns are identified. * Assist with fact\-based problem solving and prioritisation of quality issues. * Support the Quality Manager with site\-wide quality projects and initiatives. * Assist in preparing 8D (Eight Disciplines) and CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) responses for customers. * Ensure documentation is maintained in line with ISO 9001:2015 requirements. * Support continuous improvement by identifying trends and improvement opportunities. * Comply with all company policies, procedures, and governance standards. * Take reasonable care for your own Health \& Safety and that of others, reporting hazards promptly. * Carry out ad\-hoc tasks as required to support site quality objectives. **About You** * Strong understanding of quality and audit processes. * Experience with document preparation and control (SOPs and work instructions). * Advanced Excel skills with strong attention to detail. * Internal Audit certification is desirable. * Analytical mindset with strong problem\-solving skills. * Confident questioning processes and working collaboratively with operational teams. * Organised, methodical, and able to prioritise workload effectively. * Minimum of a high school diploma (or equivalent). * Previous experience working within ISO 9001:2015 environments is an advantage. **What can we offer you?** Competitive Salary Package and an opportunity to be a part of and grow within a driven and successful company, with a team of 160,000, operating in over 90 countries. In addition, we can offer access to a selection of employee benefits, such as: * Enhanced Annual Leave Entitlement from your start, which increases with length of service. * Salary Sacrifice Cycle Scheme. * Employee counselling, for support with physical and mental wellbeing. * Company Pension, which, when a member of, also provides access to Life Cover and Critical Illness cover. **Next Steps** If this sounds like the next step for you, please follow the links and apply with your CV without delay! At DSV, we celebrate the diversity of our workforce and are committed to further enhancing it. We warmly welcome applications from individuals of all genders, ages, abilities, nationalities, religions, and sexual orientations. Please be aware that all successful candidates will undergo necessary right\-to\-work checks and certain sites require 5\-years’ worth of referencing and background checks. We do not accept CV details from Recruitment Agencies unless DSV have engaged directly regarding the role requirements beforehand. **DSV – Global transport and logistics** Working at DSV means playing in a different league. As a global leader in transport and logistics, we have been on an extraordinary journey of growth. Let’s grow together as we continue to innovate, digitalise and build on our achievements. With close to 160,000 colleagues in over 90 countries, we work every day to offer solid services and meet our customers’ needs and help them achieve their goals. We know that the best way to achieve this is by bringing in new talent, fresh perspectives and ambitious individuals like you. At DSV, performance is in our DNA. We don’t just work – we aim to shape the future of logistics. This ambition fuels a dynamic environment built on collaboration with world\-class team players, accountability and action. We value inclusivity, embrace different cultures and respect the dignity and rights of every individual. If you want to make an impact, be trusted by customers and grow your career in a forward\-thinking company – this is the place to be. **Start here. Go anywhere** Visit dsv.com and follow us on LinkedIn and Facebook.

We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including: Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Key Responsibilities Of The Role** * Review and understanding project contract documentation. * Preparing and analysing supplier and sub\-contractor enquiries. * Preparation and issuing of contract documents to our supply chain. * Monitoring change in our scope of work and pricing of variations. * Preparing and agreeing Monthly progress applications. * Financial and contractual control of sub\-contractor accounts. * Issuing of contractual notices and evaluate loss and expense/acceleration claims. * Providing fast, accurate, competitive and profitable estimates. * Preparation of monthly commercial project reports. * Provide Data and analysis of issues \& risks as required by Management. * Managing and forecasting of project Cash flows. * Liaising with the client, main contractor and other departments. * All other duties associated with this role. **Essential Criteria For The Role** * Knowledge of COMM’s and ICT scope of Work and Systems * Academically (Degree) qualified and or relevant experience in the industry * 5\+ years’ experience in a QS role or engineering role with good commercial exposure * A wide understanding of subcontracts * We are looking for candidates from CSA and MEP discipline areas. * Able to negotiate with clients and subcontractors on a professional level * Experience in use of Excel a requirement (SAP experience an advantage) * Excellent Analytical and problem\-solving skills * High level of accuracy and attention to detail * Must have fluency in written and spoken English for this QS Job in the UK ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer*** **.** Määrälaskija (ICT), Suomi Mercury on Euroopan johtava rakennusratkaisujen toimittaja. Rakennamme ja hallinnoimme monimutkaisia suunnittelu\- ja rakennusprojekteja maailman johtaville yrityksille. Ratkaisumme auttavat tarjoamaan teknologioita ja elämää mullistavia edistysaskeleita, jotka yhdistävät ihmisiä, yhteisöjä ja yrityksiä ja antavat heille mahdollisuuden saavuttaa uskomattomia asioita. Ihmisillämme olla rohkeutta olla innovatiivisia. Heidän päättäväisyytensä ja tarkka keskittymisensä antavat meille mahdollisuuden toimittaa varmuudella, aika aika jälkeen. Toteutamme asiakkaidemme visioita huippuluokan rakennusratkaisuilla useilla eri aloilla, mukaan lukien: yritysten datakeskukset, edistynyt teknologia ja biotieteet, hyperskaalatut datakeskukset ja palontorjunta. Mercurylla velvollisuutemme on kannustaa ja tukea ihmisiä toteuttamaan visionsa itsestään. paikka heidät toimintamme keskiöön ja tarjoamme haastavia kehitysmahdollisuuksia loistavassa tiimissä tukevassa ympäristössä, joka antaa heille mahdollisuuden saavuttaa täyden potentiaalinsa. **Roolin Keskeiset Vastuut** * Tarkista ja ymmärrä projektisopimusdokumentaatio. * Toimittajien ja alihankkijoiden tiedustelujen valmistelu ja analysointi. * Sopimusasiakirjojen valmistelu ja toimittaminen toimitusketjullemme. * Työn laajuuden ja variaatioiden hinnoittelun muutosten seuranta. * Kuukausittaisten edistymishakemusten valmistelu ja hyväksyminen. * Alihankkijoiden tilien taloudellinen ja sopimusvalvonta. * Sopimusilmoitusten antaminen sekä tappio\- ja kulu\-/kiihtyneiden maksujen korvausvaatimusten arviointi. * Tarjoamme nopeita, tarkkoja, kilpailukykyisiä ja kannattavia kustannusarvioita. * Kuukausittaisten kaupallisten projektiraporttien laatiminen. * Tarjoa johdon edellyttämiä tietoja ja analyysejä ongelmista ja riskeistä. * Projektin kassavirtojen hallinta ja ennustaminen. * Yhteydenpito asiakkaan, pääurakoitsijan ja muiden osastojen kanssa. * Kaikki muut tähän rooliin liittyvät tehtävät. **Rooliin Liittyvät Keskeiset Kriteerit** * Tietoa viestinnän ja ICT:n työtehtävien ja järjestelmien laajuudesta * Akateeminen (tutkinto) pätevyys ja/tai asiaankuuluva kokemus teollisuus * Vähintään 5 vuoden kokemus laadunvalvonnan tai insinöörin tehtävissä ja hyvä kaupallinen kokemus * Laaja ymmärrys alihankinnoista * Etsimme ehdokkaita CSA\- ja MEP\-tieteenaloilta. * Kykyä neuvotella ammattimaisesti asiakkaiden ja alihankkijoiden kanssa * Kokemus Excelin käytöstä on välttämätöntä (SAP\-kokemus katsotaan eduksi) * Erinomaiset analyyttiset ja ongelmanratkaisutaidot * Korkea tarkkuus ja yksityiskohta huomioiminen * Tässä QS\-työpaikassa Isossa\-Britanniassa sinulla olla sujuva englanti sekä kirjallisesti että suullisesti. ***Mercury on tasa\-arvoinen työnantaja*** **.** Apply Now

title: Junior Recruitment Consultant: Fast-Track Your Career
description: Junior Recruitment Consultant: Fast\-Track Your Career * **Five Promotions in Two Years, Not the Exception, the Expectation** * **Progress Measured by Merit, Not by Time Served** * **Your Career Journey, Finally Under Your Control** You’ve mastered managing clients, meeting deadlines, and delivering exceptional results under pressure in the events sector. Now it’s time to use those skills to build a career in recruitment that moves as fast as you do. In events, recognition can take years, and sometimes it depends more on who notices than what you achieve. At Archer, your progress is entirely in your hands. Hit clear, achievable targets, and watch your career and financial earnings soar. Every milestone is mapped, every promotion earned, every success rewarded. **Why Join the Team?** * **Fast\-Track Your Career:** Use your client and people skills in recruitment to accelerate your rise. Your results drive promotions, not office politics, with the potential for up to 5 promotions in just 2 years, faster than in events. * **Be Recognised \& Rewarded:** Every deal you close and every relationship you build translates directly into career growth and financial reward. Promotions are earned based on performance, giving you real recognition for your effort. * **Take Control of Your Future:** Recruitment lets you shape your own path. Move from executing plans to driving results, influencing decisions, and building authority, all while creating the financial freedom and lifestyle you’ve been aiming for. **What You Will Do:** * **Client Management:** Build and maintain strong relationships with clients from a varied range of industries. * **Candidate Sourcing:** Proactively source, screen, and qualify candidates for various roles within the IT sector. Utilise a variety of sourcing methods, including LinkedIn, networking, and database management. * **Market Insight:** Stay informed about the IT industry, including trends, challenges, and opportunities. Use this knowledge to inform recruitment strategies and provide insights to clients and candidates. **What You Will Have:** * 3\+ years of commercial experience \- 1–3 years of experience in the events industry (Event Planner, Event Coordinator, Event Manager, PR \& Events Consultant, etc.) * A results\-driven mindset and hunger to succeed * Strong people skills, communication, and resilience **What You’ll Gain** * **Unlimited commission** with no caps * **Clear promotion roadmap** with visible milestones * **Monthly and quarterly incentives** for standout performance * **1\-to\-1 mentorship** from top performers who’ve done the journey themselves **Total Compensation for an Average Earner:** * **Year 1 – €48,000\+** * **Year 2 – €71,000\+** * **Year 2 – €100,000\+** * An uncapped salary structure means top performers earn way above this! **Ready to Turn Effort into Career Growth?** Contact Ciara O’Flynn at **ciara.oflynn@archer.ie** or **\+353 1 902 6804** to start your journey with **clear milestones, rapid progression, and a path to leadership.**

title: Junior Technician - Transportation Planning
description: **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Dublin.** Join our Transportation Planning Team to work on a large variety of impactful public and private projects undertaken by our key clients. The Transportation Planning Team works on the forefront of projects that directly face the development of sustainable communities and climate challenges. We work on high impact projects that brings global best practice, innovation, creativity and technical brilliance together. Our work builds towards Arup’s purpose which is to ‘shape a better world’ by staying committed to developing solutions that address the climate crisis, nature loss, social equity, the need to transition to more sustainable economies and to bring greater prosperity and opportunity to people’s lives. Transportation is a key economic sector impact in the creation of a resilient society and our team works at the forefront of this field to find opportunities where conventional designs can be influenced. **The Opportunity** To support our continued growth and expansion into new markets we are seeking a junior technician to join our Transportation Planning team to work collaboratively with other engineers, planners and technicians on a wide variety of projects. These projects will typically include new public realm schemes, transport infrastructure and development masterplans. The candidate will work under the leadership of a senior technician who will direct the work to be carried in a fast paced environment where the candidate will be expected to learn as work progresses. The role offers lots of opportunities to advance within the organisation both in Ireland and globally. This is an opportunity to join the Transportation Planning team on the forefront of technical design and to gain on hand experience and technical skills and knowledge alongside experts in this field. There will also be opportunity for structured training and mentorship. **As a Junior Technician At Arup, You Will** * Assist in the design and delivery of a variety of transport projects , collaborating with experienced engineers and multidisciplinary teams. Produce high\-quality detailed General Arrangement drawings, plans and sections using AutoCAD. Production of 3D models using AutoCAD Civil 3D, Revit and other BIM software * Work under instruction given by senior technician or senior engineers working within the transportation team. From time to time you might do work with other disciplines / teams within Arup. * Contribute to discussions on design options available to solve problems encountered * Assist in the development of other team members where required. * Contribute to the development of good practice and processes through involvement with the Arup skills network. * Attend and participate in, presentations and workshops. * In the long\-term, you will strive to understand the design process the engineers utilise within the team, helping the technicians explore the capabilities of various software, and developing ways in which the software can streamline the delivery of our work. **Does this role inspire you?** We're looking for graduates who is passionate about the promotion of and development of sustainable transportation and shaping a better world. **We Also Value a Candidate That Is** * Currently pursuing or recently graduated with an NFQ Level 6 Higher Certificate or Advanced Certificate q ualification in a technical qualification such as engineering, CAD, technical design or similar. * Able to demonstrate a sound technical base * Able to cooperate with others in a team environment effectively and contributes in generating ideas * An excellent communicator who brings teamwork skills and the ability to contribute effectively in a collaborative environment * Working to a plan and quality standards and meeting deadlines set * Understands how to develop self in self, in design, technical and generic skills **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in investing in our people and supporting their growth from day one. As a trust\-owned company, we're driven by our purpose and values, creating a collaborative and rewarding environment for our employees. **Benefits That Work For You** * Professional Growth \& Development: Kickstart your career with our structured graduate development program, benefit from continuous learning opportunities, and receive guidance from experienced mentors. * Global Opportunities: Collaborate with colleagues worldwide and explore potential short\-term or long\-term assignments at other Arup offices and project locations. * Financial Well\-being \& Ownership: Receive a competitive graduate salary and benefits package and share in Arup's success through our global profit\-sharing program. * Wellbeing and Culture: At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme. * Equality, Diversity and Inclusion: We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. \#IREGrad **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?**
