title: Labs CSV Engineer
description: **Labs CSV Engineer \- Ireland, Dublin \- 12 Month Contract** Our client is a global leader in pharmaceutical innovation, specialising in the development and manufacture of cutting\-edge drugs. As they continue to grow, they’re seeking an experienced Labs CSV Engineer to join their dynamic team and drive forward groundbreaking projects in Dublin. **What You’ll Do** * Partner with local project teams to deliver Automation and IT aspects of Laboratory capital projects. * Develop and/or review System Development Life Cycle (SDLC) deliverables, compliant with company standards. * Liaise with site AIT group to add input to site SOPs for lab systems. * AuthorReviewApproval of qualification documents for lab systems – RS, CS, RTM etc. * Work with site teams on generation of requirement andor configuration specification and IOQ test protocols. Participate in execution of IOQ protocols for lab analytical instruments where required. ******What you’ll need:****** * BSc in Engineering, Computer Science, and or other technical degree. * Extensive experience in project/automation/IT/CSV/IT and or a similar role with experience in laboratory analytical instrument qualification and system implementation. * Excellent facilitation, problem solving, collaboration and decision\-making skills. * Ability to multitask and to handle tasks with competing priorities effectively and engage with stakeholders. ****If this role is of interest to you, please apply now!****

title: Lawyer – Corporate (Insolvency Focus)
description: We are working with a top\-tier national law firm based in Dublin for an Associate to join their Corporate Insolvency team. **The Role:** The successful candidate will join a leading Corporate M\&A team, advising domestic and international clients on complex and innovative transactions across the full corporate lifecycle. They will work on public and private M\&A, joint ventures, reorganisations, private equity transactions, and corporate governance matters. They will provide commercially focused legal advice, draft and negotiate key transaction documents, and build strong client relationships while delivering a high standard of service. The role also involves contributing to business development and thought leadership initiatives. **Essential Criteria:** * 2\+ years’ PQE; * Strong corporate law background; * Excellent academics; * Analytical; * Technically excellent; * Strong communication skills; * Flexible and collaborative team player. **Benefits:** * Excellent salary package (commensurate with experience); * Hybrid working; * Generous annual leave; * Professional development; * Friendly culture. Please contact Andrew Currie on 0892015064 or email andrew.currie@achuman.com if you would like to have a confidential discussion about this opportunity.

title: Logistics Specialist
description: **Location:** Ireland (Hybrid) **Salary:** €40,000\- €46,000 (depending on experience) **About Sycomp** Sycomp is a Global Systems Integrator headquartered in Silicon Valley, delivering end‑to‑end IT lifecycle services including logistics, integration, managed services, and global field support for enterprise and Fortune 500 customers. With operations across the Americas, EMEA, and APAC, Sycomp enables customers to deploy and support IT infrastructure at scale with speed, quality, and compliance. Our Logistics team plays a critical role in supporting global device lifecycle programs, ensuring seamless outbound and return logistics, strong carrier management, and on‑time, in‑full delivery across regions. **About the Role** We are seeking a **Logistics Specialist** to join our Ireland‑based Logistics team. This role is responsible for managing outbound and return shipments, carrier coordination, shipment tracking, and logistics administration supporting Managed Service Provider (MSP) and parcel shipments from the Ireland warehouse. The successful candidate will work closely with internal teams, external carriers, and local vendors to ensure accurate documentation, proactive shipment tracking, cost control, and high service levels. **Key Responsibilities** * Full ownership of MSP return logistics, including customer communication, case management, and administration * Issue Air Waybills (AWBs) for outbound MSP shipments, including quality assurance checks of Commercial Invoices (CIs) for exports (e.g., Salesforce) * Maintain accurate case updates with tracking details across all shipments * Perform live tracking of outbound shipments to proactively identify and mitigate delays, manage exceptions, and support OTIF (On‑Time In‑Full) delivery * Act as the primary point of contact for DHL Ireland, managing day‑to‑day carrier interactions * Maintain MSP tracking files, including weekly cost logging and reconciliation of DHL and Omni UK invoices * Arrange and manage E‑waste and disposal requests with approved local vendors * Own and manage locally created Service Requests (SRs) for parcel shipments, with ongoing training on pallet shipments and progression toward full ownership * Perform Oracle Ship Confirm for all outbound orders from the Ireland warehouse * Support additional logistics‑related tasks as assigned by the Logistics Manager **Requirements** * 3\+ years of experience in logistics, supply chain, distribution, or shipping coordination roles * Hands‑on experience issuing AWBs and managing parcel and/or palletised shipments * Familiarity with carrier management and shipment tracking, ideally with DHL or similar global couriers * Working knowledge of export documentation, including Commercial Invoices * Strong administrative and organisational skills with high attention to detail * Experience using logistics, ERP, or order management systems (Oracle experience is an advantage) * Ability to manage multiple shipments concurrently and proactively resolve delivery issues * Comfortable communicating with internal stakeholders, customers, and external vendors * Based in Ireland, or able to work Ireland hours, with hybrid working flexibility *Sycomp is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, color, age, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability status, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic.* *Sycomp will provide reasonable accommodation for qualified individuals with disabilities as needed. If you need assistance or an accommodation in applying, please contact our Human Resources Department at careers@sycomp.com* *Sycomp may use automated tools, including artificial intelligence, to support certain aspects of the recruitment process, such as reviewing applications or identifying relevant experience. These tools assist our teams, and all hiring decisions are made following human review*

title: Maintenance Technician
description: Opportunity for **Electrical Maintenance Technician** to join Operations \& Maintenance team based in Dublin. This is a **DAY SHIFT** role. **Overview of the Role:** The electrical utilities engineering technician will work as part of the Dalkia Maintenance and Calibration team on a major client Biopharmaceutical site. He/she will liaise with the contract team, client engineering and equipment vendors to ensure maximum uptime of all utilities plant and equipment, and that equipment is in compliance with all regulatory, statutory and client corporate requirements. The electrical technician will train on all Processes and techniques across Predictive Maintenance, CMMS (Pemac), Reliability techniques, MV distribution, Turbines, Boilers Compressors, Diesel Generators Pumps etc. Duties of the Role include: * Coordinate the day to day work with shift technicians on\-site particularly morning shift changeover * Trouble\-shooting and high\-level investigation of utility plant equipment faults and failures \- root cause analysis * Compliance/quality engineering \- recording and investigation of deviations on utility plant and equipment * Carry out planned preventive, predictive and corrective maintenance as required * Good knowledge and understanding of CMMS systems * Review maintenance programme periodically to ensure optimal uptime levels are achieved * Provide leadership and mentoring to the team in developing their capability to resolve complex plant deviations and failures * Support the Site engineer in carrying out Root Cause Analysis on plant failures to determine the sequence of events, the underlying root cause and assist the in developing proposals to remedy the fault; * Provide support to capital improvement projects on utilities systems, and be involved in the commissioning and hand over from projects to operations. * Assist the Site Engineer in planning and executing complex utility service isolations for maintenance, projects and client works; * Identify, develop and, if approved, implement Continuous Improvement measures to improve the efficiency of plant and working practices; **Required Skills for the Role:** * Electrical engineering degree or diploma desirable * 5\+ years industrial experience in a similar role preferable * Senior Trades, City \& Guilds or equivalent Please forward updated CV's to aidan@synergyrecruitment.ie

title: Market Operator
description: We are the people behind Ireland’s electricity grid. We operate the power system with world leading levels of variable asynchronous renewable generation, ensuring that everyone has power when they need it. But that’s not all, we are now transforming the power system for future generations. And that’s just the beginning of our story. We have a critical purpose. That purpose is to transform the power system to enable our society to reduce our carbon emissions and meet our climate change and net zero ambitions. Be part of the transformation! **The Team** The Market Operations Function is responsible for operating and enhancing the European integrated Single Electricity Market across the island of Ireland that allows generators and supply companies to buy and sell electricity ahead of the delivery date and subsequently the calculation of prices that apply for the real time balancing of supply and demand. This includes the operation of the day\-ahead and intraday markets and the Balancing Market Operations. Analysing market behaviour is a significant activity, given the scale of money being transacted, \~ €5bn per annum, and the size of datasets generated. The markets operate on a 365\-day basis and generate significant datasets down to five\-minute resolution. The Market Operations Function want to recruit individuals who are interested in advancing their career in the energy industry. The critical nature of the work we do, and the experience it offers, provides exciting career opportunity. **Job Purpose** The Market Operator plays a key role in delivery of the full range of work within the Market Operations team, which includes operating and monitoring the trading desk on a shift rota arrangement 1 week in every 4\-6 weeks. Upon joining a comprehensive 3 month training programme is provided to become licensed and following completion of the training, the Market Operator is certified to operate the electricity market auctions as part of the trading desk team. During electricity market auctions the trading team have specific tasks that need to be completed within pre\-determined timelines and the team work to deadlines which can be in real\-time, daily, weekly, monthly. As a result, the Market Operator needs to work closely with the other team members to ensure the appropriate support and backup is available within this fast\-paced environment. The Market Operator works closely with service providers to operate and verify the results from the various wholesale markets. The Market Operator must maintain oversight of all market operations auction activities during the auctions and liaise closely with stakeholders to resolve operational issues, typically to very tight deadlines. These stakeholders include the National Control Centre, Market Operations management and internal IT, in addition to interacting with external parties including service providers, traders, the UK system operator, interconnector owners and other European/UK market operators. When the Market Operator is not working shift on the trading desk, the role involves a blend of responsibilities for projects, system release testing, service changes, market analysis, training and process improvement. **Key Responsibilities** * Perform key daily checks and ensure market system processes operate smoothly and where issues are identified, seek to resolve the issue within agreed parameters or escalate as necessary * Where resolution of issues is not clear, use experience and knowledge to identify potential solutions and engage with senior team members to address * Engage with internal and/or external stakeholders to address market operation queries * Monitoring key market activities such as auctions and publication of reports to ensure accuracy and timeliness * Using excel skills, investigate any errors that have been flagged on the market system and capture relevant as necessary * Rectify errors during market auctions using agreed procedures and engage with internal and external stakeholders as appropriate to ensure smooth operation * Monitor the market system inputting data and validating outputs to ensure accuracy. * Engage with customers where data is missing and provide workarounds for data submission. * Publish urgent market messages via email to external stakeholders as required * Raise incident tickets on the system and coordinate internal and external parties to resolve issues * Proactive participation in daily incident committee meetings with European Market Operators representing EirGrid as appropriate * Prepare a handover of shift notes following each shift to the Market Operator next on the trading desk * Run reports and quality check before ensuring reports have been published on relevant website * Work closely with the National Control Centre and Castlereagh House Control Centre to resolve issues where they arise in real\-time and escalate to Team Lead as appropriate * Perform user acceptance testing of system releases (full training provided) * Review and enhance processes, procedures, and checklists to provide continuous improvement in management of operational risks, efficiency execution of activities and adherence with the latest operating obligations. * Perform analysis on market outcomes and produce reports for both internal and external stakeholders. * Identify, develop and implement ongoing service changes and improvements, process improvement and training activities * Ensure compliance with all regulatory requirements in performing market auction activities * Provide standby availability cover 7 days per month from 7am to 7pm in case issues arise on the trading desk and additional resources are required. **Knowledge, Skills, And Experience** * Typically 3 years experience in a data and /or critical operational role. * Strong numerical and analytical ability * Effective time management to achieve strict deadlines in a fast\-paced environment * Strong attention to detail * Intermediate level Excel * Experience using multiple systems at the same time and ability to learn and operate various systems in order to monitor and troubleshoot process execution * Excellent communication skills and confidence to work cross functionally in a business environment * Ability to work effectively within a team environment under time pressures with a flexible work approach * Strong skills in MS Office products and file management systems e.g. SharePoint **Closing date for applications** 29th May 2026 We value potential as much as experience. If you believe you can succeed in this role, we encourage you to apply, even if you don’t meet every requirement. We're dedicated to diversity and inclusion at Eirgrid. We recognise the strength that comes from having a diverse workforce and the importance of a supportive culture for all our people to achieve their potential. Our diversity and inclusion networks have been designed to create an inclusive culture that enables our people to feel a sense of belonging at work. As an equal opportunities’ employer, we welcome applications from people of all backgrounds. Reasonable accommodations are offered at every stage of our recruitment process.

title: Marketing Data Analyst
description: **Why Arup?** At Arup we’re dedicated to shaping a better world. Our purpose, shared values and collaborative approach have set us apart for over 80 years. We solve the world's most complex problems and deliver what seems impossible, with curiosity and creativity. We’re a thriving and growing organisation which offers you many possibilities to shape a better world, and your future. In an increasingly competitive marketplace, our Marketing, Communications, and Business Development (MCBD) team enables Arup to build and protect our reputation, deepen client relationships, win quality work, and attract top talent. This role will be key in achieving our strategic market and client ambitions. We are seeking to hire a Marketing Data Analyst to strengthen our MCBD function. **The Opportunity** This role is within the evolving global business development team. You will be working with business development personnel across the firm, including those responsible for the development of client systems and those running business development programmes such as the client programme, the contractor programme and initiatives identified by the Business and Markets Executive / Business Growth and Client Systems leadership. The winning of work and associated activities are key to the success of Arup, more than ever before. This is an opportunity to be part of the team enabling firm growth. **Key Responsibilities** * Develop and assist in the development of all forms of Power BI reporting utilizing CRM, finance and other data for use by the marketing, communications, business development and Business and Markets communities across Arup. * Work with leadership, MCBD and other members of the Client Systems team to define requirements for reporting, to sketch out possible solutions, and to elicit feedback once reporting has been created. * Support the coordination of the Marketing Data Warehouse. Work with our digital marketing experts on our data warehouse that stores data from our digital marketing platform, Marketo, and its evolution to encompass website and social media data. Its aim is to give a complete picture of the operation of our digital marketing channels. * Bring your expertise in reporting to support the development of CRM and other tools so that our data is best configured to produce the reporting we need. * Explore the use of AI to support development of reporting solutions as well as generate insights into our business growth department. * Collaborate with the wider ARUP’s analytics community in predictive modeling and statistical analysis projects that would help surface insights around improving our work\-winning processes. **Is this role right for me?** **Requirements** * Experience in the use of Power BI plus high\-level Excel skills. Desire to grow these skills. Potential interest in languages such as Python and tools such as Databricks and SQL querying. * Experience working with CRM and/or finance data. Knowledge of how to clean data prior to processing. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills. Ability to think laterally to suggest potential solutions. * Strong interpersonal and communication skills, both written and verbal. **Qualifications And Accreditations** * Degree, postgraduate qualification in Information Technology, Computer Science, Knowledge Management or a related field. **Knowledge And Experience** * Experience in data management, knowledge management and analytics. * Knowledge of client systems and software development. * Proficiency in marketing and communications related technologies, and knowledge of marketing data including bidding and clients data. * Experience in building trusted relationships with internal stakeholders. * Knowledge of current trends in AI, data information, machine learning and data quality is a plus. **Skills And Attributes** * Skills in strategic thinking and analysis and problem\-solving. * Communication and interpersonal skills. * Ability to work in a fast\-paced, dynamic environment. * Ability to work under pressure and manage multiple priorities. * Able to build collaborative and inclusive working relationships with peers, leaders and other stakeholders. * High level of personal integrity, professionalism, and inclusive leadership. * Commitment to exemplifying Arup’s aims, particularly those of being a ‘humane organisation’, maintaining ‘quality of work’, and ‘straight and honourable dealings. **What We Offer You** At Arup, we care about each member’s success, so we can grow together. Guided by our values, we provide an attractive total reward package that recognizes the contribution of each of our members to our shared success. As well as competitive, fair and equitable pay, we offer a career in which all of our members can belong, grow and thrive – through benefits that support health and wellbeing, a wide range of learning opportunities and many possibilities to have an impact through the work they do. We are owned in trust on behalf of our members, giving us the freedom, with personal responsibility, to set our own direction and choose work that aligns with our purpose and adds to Arup’s legacy. Our members collaborate on ambitious projects to deliver remarkable outcomes for our clients and communities. Profit Share is a key part of our reward, enabling members to share in the results of our collective efforts. We also provide Private medical insurance, Life assurance, Accident insurance and Income protection cover. In addition, you’ll have access to flexible benefits to help you look after all aspects of your wellbeing and give you the freedom and flexibility to find the best solutions for you, your family, and your individual needs. **Different People, Shared Values** Arup is an equal opportunity employer that actively promotes and nurtures a diverse and inclusive workforce. We welcome applications from individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, marital status, disability, neurotype or mental health, race or ethnicity, faith or belief, sexual orientation, socioeconomic background, and whether you’re pregnant or on family leave. We are an open environment that embraces diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas – this drives our excellence. Guided by our values and alignment with the UN Sustainable Development Goals, we create and contribute to equitable spaces and systems, while cultivating a sense of belonging for all. Our internal employee networks support our inclusive culture: from race, ethnicity and cross\-cultural working to gender equity and LGBTQ\+ and disability inclusion – we aim to create a space for you to express yourself and make a positive difference. Discover more about life at Arup at www.arup.com/careers/your\-life\-at\-arup. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible to all candidates. Please contact nick.newell@arup.com to let us know if you need any assistance or reasonable adjustments throughout your application or interview process, and/or to perform the essential functions of the role. We will do everything we can to support you. **Our Application Process** To understand what to expect next, please visit: https://www.arup.com/careers/recruitment\-process/ Stay safe online – Arup will never ask for your bank details as part of our recruitment process. Recruitment Agencies – We have a Preferred Supplier List of trusted partners to assist us when required and do not acknowledge any speculative CVs or unsolicited candidate introductions from agencies not on the list.

title: Marketing – Graduate Programme
description: **Description** Ryanair Labs is the technology wing of Ryanair, Europe’s Largest Airline Group. Based in our state\-of\-the\-art digital and IT innovation hub, Labs is focused on creating Europe’s leading travel experience for millions of customers across the Ryanair network. Our Labs teams consist of software development, BI \& data, cybersecurity, infrastructure, QA, project planning \& delivery and GEN AI professionals. As a Marketing Graduate, you will help bring this work to life by showcasing the people, projects and innovation happening across Ryanair Labs. \*Start date will be September 2026\* **The Role** We are delighted to announce that applications are now open for our 2026 Marketing Graduate Programme for Ryanair Labs. This is an exciting opportunity for a creative, organised and ambitious marketing graduate to join a fast\-paced technology environment and support the promotion of Ryanair Labs across internal and external channels. While you will collaborate with different marketing and internal teams, this will be a standalone position giving you ownership of the marketing strategy. Key Responsibilities * As a Marketing Graduate, you will support the Ryanair Labs team across a wide range of marketing and communications activities, including: * Creating engaging marketing content to promote Ryanair Labs, our people, projects and technology teams * Producing short\-form video content for social media, careers campaigns, events and internal communications * Filming and editing videos using tools such as CapCut and Canva * Supporting the planning and delivery of social media campaigns * Assisting with copywriting for social posts, newsletters, blogs, careers pages and internal updates * Helping to develop employer branding content that showcases life at Ryanair Labs * Capturing photo and video content at Labs events, graduate and team initiatives * Working with stakeholders to gather stories, interviews, project updates and content ideas * Bringing fresh ideas to help improve how Ryanair Labs communicates its culture and technology work * Assisting with event marketing, including careers fairs, university events, internal showcases and technology meetups. **Requirements** * Graduated with a minimum 2\.1 degree in Marketing or a related discipline * Graduated/finished university no earlier than 2024 * Experience creating short\-form video content for social media platforms * Video filming and editing skills, ideally using tools such as CapCut and Canva. * Strong copywriting skills with the ability to adapt tone of voice for different audiences * Good visual eye for content, layout, branding and storytelling * Strong organisational skills and the ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines * Confidence working with different teams and stakeholders to gather content and ideas * Ability to work on your own initiative and bring new ideas to the table * Strong interpersonal and communication skills * Good PC skills, including Microsoft Word, Excel and PowerPoint * A positive attitude, willingness to learn and a good sense of humour — doesn’t take themselves too seriously! **Benefits** * Competitive salary * Discounted and unlimited travel to over 250 destinations * Defined Contribution Pension Scheme – Matched up to 5% or €5,000 * Death in Service Benefit – Up to 2 times of annual basic salary * 20 Days Annual Leave – Increasing to 22 days after 12 months and 25 days after 3 years of continuous service. * Option for up to 5 additional unpaid leave days per year * Cycle 2 Work Scheme * Unrivalled career progression Competencies Adaptability/Flexibility Creativity/Innovation Dependability Initiative Communication Interpersonal Skills

title: Microsoft Dynamics 365 CE Developer
description: **eir business talent** is recruiting **a Microsoft Dynamics 365 Developer** on behalf of our client **.** This is a full\-time **Permanent opportunity** based in the **Citywest office** operating on a **hybrid model** with **2–3 days onsite** per week **or remotely within the UK.** The successful candidate will support the delivery, enhancement and ongoing development of Dynamics 365 Customer Engagement solutions within a fast\-paced and innovative enterprise environment. **About the Role** This role is ideal for a developer with strong experience in Dynamics 365 Customer Engagement (CE), particularly within Customer Service environments. You will play a key role in enhancing existing capabilities while also contributing to the design and delivery of new greenfield Dynamics 365 Customer Service solutions. Working as part of a collaborative transformation team, you will help deliver scalable, secure, and innovative business solutions that support continued growth. **Key Responsibilities** * Configure and maintain Dynamics 365 CE solutions, with a focus on Customer Service environments. * Work effectively within a managed Dynamics 365 environment. * Design, build, and maintain Power Automate flows to streamline and automate business processes. * Deploy solutions through CI/CD pipelines (manual deployments to test and production environments are not permitted). * Manage and maintain Git\-based source control and versioning practices. * Support the CRM Architect with solution documentation, governance, configuration oversight, and integrations. **Dynamics 365 CRM Development** * Configure CRM components including: + Tables + Views + Forms + Business Process Flows + Security Roles + Workflows * Develop plugins and JavaScript libraries. * Support the development and maintenance of Canvas Apps where required. * Stay current with new Dynamics 365 and Power Platform features and capabilities. **Project Delivery** * Work within project and programme objectives and delivery guidelines. * Participate in Agile sprint ceremonies and delivery processes. * Prioritise tasks effectively and provide regular progress and status updates to relevant project streams. **Requirements** * 5\+ years’ experience in Dynamics 365 development, ideally within Customer Engagement (CE/CRM). * Strong expertise in Dynamics 365 Customer Service and the Microsoft Power Platform. * Experience designing reusable, scalable solutions and components. * Hands\-on experience developing: + Plugins + Workflows + Power Automate flows + Logic Apps + Canvas Apps * Experience designing integrations using Microsoft Azure. * Familiarity with ALM tools such as Git Enterprise and Azure DevOps. * Strong troubleshooting and problem\-solving skills within cloud and solution architecture environments. * Ability to collaborate effectively with cross\-functional teams. * Proven ability to analyse complex technical challenges and implement best\-practice solutions. **Desirable Experience** * Experience building web applications in Azure. * SAP integration experience. * Experience working with SaaS platforms and integrations. Candidates should hold appropriate RTWs for Ireland i.e. Stamp1G Spousal, Stamp 4, Irish/British/EU passport. For more information please apply here or contact harleen.tora@eirbusiness.ie **Ref:** EET8071 Candidates must be eligible to work full time and long term in the location specified or currently hold a valid appropriate long term work Visa to apply. **eir business talent** , **eir business** and our clients are equal opportunity employers who seeks to recruit and appoint the best available person for a job regardless of marital / civil partnership status, sex (including pregnancy), age, religion, belief, race, nationality and ethnic or national origin, colour, sexual orientation or disability. **eir business talent** , **eir business** and our clients apply all relevant Data Protection laws when processing your Personal Data. If you choose to apply to this opportunity and share your CV or other personal information with **eir business talent,** **eir business** and our clients, these details will be held by us in accordance with our privacy policy used by our recruitment team to contact you regarding this or other relevant opportunities at **eir business talent** and **eir business** .

title: Microsoft Dynamics Consultant
description: My Dublin based client is recruiting for a Microsoft Dynamics 365 Developer to join the team on a perm basis working on a hybrid model. The Microsoft Dynamics 365 Developer will work on a large scale transformation programme and you will support delivery across several strategics initiatives. * Deliver and support Dynamics 365 Customer Engagement solutions within customer service\-focused environments. * Configure and enhance Dynamics 365 applications in line with operational and business requirements. * Build and optimise automated workflows using Power Automate to improve efficiency and reduce manual processes. * Support structured release management processes using CI/CD pipelines across multiple environments. * Maintain source control standards and collaborative development practices using Git\-based repositories. * Contribute to technical documentation, environment governance, and integration activities. * Configure and customise core CRM elements including entities/tables, forms, dashboards, views, workflows, business process flows, and security models. * Develop custom extensions using plugins, JavaScript, and related technologies where required. * Assist with the design and support of Power Platform solutions, including Canvas Apps. * Keep current with emerging Microsoft technologies, platform updates, and best practices. **Delivery \& Project Responsibilities** * Contribute to project delivery activities in line with agreed timelines, priorities, and objectives. * Participate in Agile ceremonies and work effectively within sprint\-based delivery models. * Manage assigned tasks proactively and communicate progress, risks, and dependencies to stakeholders. * Collaborate closely with technical teams, business users, and project stakeholders to support successful solution delivery. **Skills \& Experience** * Minimum of 5 years’ experience delivering Microsoft Dynamics 365 CE/CRM solutions. * Strong understanding of Dynamics 365 Customer Service and the wider Microsoft Power Platform ecosystem. * Experience designing scalable, maintainable, and reusable technical solutions. * Hands\-on experience developing plugins, workflows, Power Automate solutions, Logic Apps, and Canvas Apps. * Knowledge of Azure integration services and cloud\-based solution design. * teams. * Experience with Azure\-based web applications or enterprise system integrations would be beneficial. * Good understanding of SaaS applications, APIs, and integration approaches.

title: Night Porter
description: You will receive training through our Dalata Academy to help you learn important skills. You will also meet experienced team members who can help you grow. At Dalata, we believe in promoting from within. If you work hard, you can have new opportunities in the future. Join us and be part of a friendly team! **Your Job** * Check in and out hotel guests as required, as well as assist with guest requests in relation to luggage/porterage. * Responsible for the cleaning of areas in the hotel, including but not restricted to the lobby, back office, stairways, restaurant and bar floor, public and staff areas and the meeting room floor. * Responsible for cleaning other areas as directed by the manager on duty and also as per the nightly checklist, which may include the set\-up of function rooms, the breakfast area, etc. * Responsibility for the front entrance and outside of the hotel and ensuring that it is clean and tidy at all times. This includes emptying bins, picking up rubbish etc. * To have a full understanding of the hotels’ fire procedures to ensure the safety of all staff and guests at all times. * Responsibility for setting up meeting rooms. **What You’ll Need** * Experience in working with customers * Good spoken English * Strong organizational skills * Able to work in a busy environment * Be a good team player **Benefits** * Free employee meals on duty * 50% Off F\&B in all properties * €/£60 employee rate on rooms for all Dalata owned hotels * 30% off the Friends \& Family rate * Extra AL day for every year worked (5 days to a maximum of 5 days) * Employee Assistance Programme. * Cycle to Work Scheme/Transport Schemes * Pension access (Ask your HR Manager for local pension information) * Development Opportunities through our Dalata Academy\- support your career journey * Staff Appreciation Initiatives, Fun Team Building Activities and regular charity events **About Us** Dalata Hotel Group – We bring *the heart of hospitality* to every hotel we own and operate. We see our growth and expansion plans as a way of spreading our unique way of caring for our guests further around the world, bringing better experiences to more people. Currently, the Dalata difference can be felt at each of our hotels in Ireland, the UK, Germany \& the Netherlands, and we’ll keep expanding this further, focusing on key UK cities, Dublin and Continental Europe. Helping our people to grow and develop their careers is vitally important to us and is one of our highest priorities. We have many exciting and unique internal development programmes, each crafted to make sure our employees get the learning they need to be able to take the next step in their careers. Dalata Hotel Group is an Inclusive Employer. We actively encourage people from all backgrounds and abilities to apply to be part of our team. However, we understand everyone's needs are different; therefore, if you require any accommodations with this application process, please highlight your needs when you are completing your application process.

title: Office Administrator
description: **Office Administrator** **West Dublin Office\-based (5 days per week)** **€35,000 – €40,000 DOE** A well\-established security solutions company based in South Dublin are hiring an organised, confident and highly detail\-focused Office Administrator to join their busy operations team. You’ll work closely with the Office Manager and play a vital part in ensuring technicians are scheduled efficiently, customers are looked after professionally, and jobs are managed accurately from start to finish. You’ll be the first point of contact for many customers, including property management companies and commercial clients and the role will involves coordinating and scheduling technicians based on their location, technical specialism and workload, while ensuring customers receive a prompt and professional service. You’ll manage incoming calls, log and update jobs on the CRM system, record technician updates and job outcomes, and provide day\-to\-day administrative support to the wider team. Strong communication skills, excellent attention to detail and the ability to prioritise in a busy environment are essential. It is expected that you have prior experience working in an office environment with excellent attention to detail and communication skills

Reporting to the Head of Risk, you can expect strong exposure and autonomy in this role. If you do not hear back within 72 hours, you can assume you have not been shortlisted on this occasion.

title: Operations Co-Ordinator
description: **About parkrun** parkrun is a health and wellbeing charity and our vision is to create a healthier and happier planet. Since our inception in 2004, parkrun has evolved into a global social movement with a vibrant and inclusive community at its core. As of 2026, we have recorded over 150\-million instances of walking, jogging, running, and volunteering. Our mission is to transform lives by empowering people to be active, social and outdoors in their local community; and to protect parkrun for the generations to come. Each and every weekend, we deliver free, weekly timed events that people of all ages and backgrounds can join in. By tackling barriers to participation, we encourage communities to come together and embrace regular physical activity. Now in its 22nd year, parkrun spans over 3,200 locations in 23 countries with more than 12 million people registered, and counting. And we have really only just begun. **Brief role description:** This role sits at the heart of the Irish team operations, providing seamless coordination across events, administration, and systems. Acting as a key point of contact, the role ensures queries are managed efficiently, logistics are delivered smoothly, and new event processes are well coordinated, while maintaining strong compliance standards. **Job Description \- Operations Coordinator, ROI** Main Responsibilities: Administration \& Systems Coordination * Deliver high\-quality general office support, ensuring the team operates smoothly on a day\-to\-day basis * Act as a key point of contact for incoming queries across the ROI team, managing requests via Zendesk with efficiency and professionalism * Maintain and update global systems with a high degree of accuracy and attention to detail * Support the onboarding and ongoing updates of Ambassadors and Event Directors (EDs) across ROI. Events \& Stakeholder Coordination * Respond to and manage event\-related queries, ensuring timely resolution and clear communication * Support the coordination of requirements for new events, ensuring all administrative and operational needs are captured and delivered * Coordinate meet\-and\-greet events, managing logistics in collaboration with EDs and Ambassadors across ROI, and ensuring accurate meeting minutes are recorded and distributed. Operations, Compliance \& Safeguarding * Ensure all materials and resources are in place to support events and team activities * Administer volunteer expenses and agreements, ensuring accuracy, timely processing, and secure record\-keeping * Oversee safeguarding administration, including Garda vetting, record\-keeping, and tracking volunteer vetting and marshal compliance across junior events, in collaboration with our vetting partner * Support the ongoing review and improvement of policies and procedures, ensuring they are clearly communicated and consistently followed * Uphold compliance standards across all events and operational activities * Prepare documentation and risk assessments for incidents or cases requiring further review **Essential experience requirements:** * Strong organisational and administrative experience, with the ability to manage multiple priorities effectively * Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills * Experience working across multiple systems with high levels of accuracy and attention to detail * Ability to handle sensitive information with professionalism and discretion * Strong problem\-solving skills and a proactive, solutions\-focused approach * Calm and adaptable under pressure, with the ability to manage competing demands * A collaborative, positive approach with strong ownership and accountability **Desirable experience requirements:** * Experience of working with and supporting volunteers * Experience in events, operations, or coordination roles * Familiarity with customer support or ticketing systems (e.g. Zendesk) * Experience working in a community, non\-profit, or volunteer\-led environment * Exposure to compliance, safeguarding, or governance processes * Understanding of the parkrun and parkrun junior operating model **Professional certification requirements:** N/a

title: PLM Developer
description: Anduril Industries is a defense technology company with a mission to transform U.S. and allied military capabilities with advanced technology. By bringing the expertise, technology, and business model of the 21st century’s most innovative companies to the defense industry, Anduril is changing how military systems are designed, built and sold. Anduril’s family of systems is powered by Lattice OS, an AI\-powered operating system that turns thousands of data streams into a realtime, 3D command and control center. As the world enters an era of strategic competition, Anduril is committed to bringing cutting\-edge autonomy, AI, computer vision, sensor fusion, and networking technology to the military in months, not years. **About The Team** The Voyager team in Anduril Industries is an engineering and design division with a vast experience in developing innovative computing and communications solutions for the network edge. The team specializes in integrating enterprise networking capabilities from global IT leaders with purpose\-built hardware and software platforms designed to meet market demands for the most stringent of environmental requirements. We are seeking a skilled PLM Developer with knowledge in PTC Windchill to join our IT Systems team in Dublin. This role is responsible for maintaining and improving PLM custom solutions and related applications and integrations. You will be working on supporting users, troubleshooting, data migration and maintenance of backend configurations within our PLM ecosystem to align with evolving business and engineering requirements. You will work closely with cross\-functional teams, including engineering, IT, and operations, to ensure seamless PLM system functionality, data integrity, and process automation. **Key Responsibilities** * Maintain existing backend configuration and customizations in PLM systems, specifically using PTC Windchill and Temcenter. * Maintain integration of PLM systems with other enterprise applications (ERP, CAD, XPLM, etc.). * Troubleshoot issues and provide technical support to end\-users. * Support data migration of legacy products from old system configuration to new * Collaborate with internal teams and external vendors to optimize PLM capabilities * Create and maintain comprehensive documentation for code, applications, and customizations to ensure future support and clarity, ensuring smooth deployment * Provide ongoing support for deployed solutions, addressing user\-reported issues and implementing necessary updates * Participate in system upgrades, testing, and validation. * Analyze quantitative and qualitative data to identify root cause of issues to determine the opportunity size and business impact. * Support PLM System migration **Required Qualifications** * Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field. * Strong programming skills in Java/J2EE technologies – JavaScript, GWT, JQuery, XML, JSPs, SQL Teamcenter etc. * Proven development experience of 3\+ years with PTC Windchill or other similar software, including system development and administration. * Knowledge and experience in Linux environments * Knowledge \& experience in Build and Release process * Experience with PLM customization frameworks, APIs, and data models. * Strong understanding of product data management and change control processes. * Familiarity with integration between PLM and CAD/ERP systems such as SAP B1 and related SAP BPA’s * Proven track record of partnering with stakeholders while employing a high degree of collaboration and influence. * Excellent analytical and problem\-solving skills * Strong written and verbal communication skills **Preferred Qualifications** * Knowledge of enterprise integration middleware (e.g., PTC ThingWorx or custom APIs). * Agile development and DevOps exposure. * Experience with system migration or PLM system transitions **Benefits** The salary range for this role is an estimate based on a wide range of compensation factors, inclusive of base salary only. Actual salary offer may vary based on (but not limited to) work experience, education and/or training, critical skills, and/or business considerations. Highly competitive equity grants are included in the majority of full time offers; and are considered part of Anduril's total compensation package. Additionally, Anduril offers top\-tier benefits for full\-time employees, including: At Anduril, we invest in our people. Our comprehensive, competitive benefits package (available at little to no cost to employees) ensures you’re supported in health, recovery, and whatever comes next. *For more information, Explore Our Benefits.* **Protecting Yourself from Recruitment Scams** Anduril is committed to maintaining the integrity of our Talent acquisition process and the security of our candidates. We've observed a rise in sophisticated phishing and fraudulent schemes where individuals impersonate Anduril representatives, luring job seekers with false interviews or job offers. These scammers often attempt to extract payment or sensitive personal information. To Ensure Your Safety And Help You Navigate Your Job Search With Confidence, Please Keep The Following Critical Points In Mind * No Financial Requests: Anduril will never solicit payment or demand personal financial details (such as banking information, credit card numbers, or social security numbers) at any stage of our hiring process. Our legitimate recruitment is entirely free for candidates. * Please always verify communications: + Direct from Anduril: If you receive an email from one of our recruiters, it will only come from an @anduril.com address. + Via Agency Partner: If contacted by a recruiting agency for an Anduril role, their email will clearly identify their agency. If you suspect any suspicious activity, please verify the agency's authenticity by reaching out to contact@anduril.com. * Exercise Caution with Unsolicited Outreach: If you receive any communication that appears suspicious, contains grammatical errors, or makes unusual requests, do not engage. Always confirm the sender's email domain is @anduril.com before providing any personal information or clicking on links. * What to Do If You Suspect Fraud: Should you encounter any questionable or fraudulent outreach claiming to be from Anduril, please report it immediately to contact@anduril.com. Your proactive caution is invaluable in protecting your personal information and upholding the security and trustworthiness of our recruitment efforts. **Data Privacy** To view Anduril's candidate data privacy policy, please visit https://anduril.com/applicant\-privacy\-notice/. By submitting your application, you consent to Anduril Industries using a third\-party service provider to conduct pre\-employment risk, integrity, and due diligence screening and assessing potential risks as part of your application process. This third\-party service provider provides risk\-intelligence services that may include analysis of sanctions and watchlists, adverse media, public\-record information, and other lawful open\-source or commercial data sources. This third\-party service provider does not act as a consumer reporting agency. Use of this provider helps to ensure compliance with applicable laws and protect technology, intellectual property, and organizational security.

title: People Technology Integration Engineer (Workday)
description: **Why Sony Interactive Entertainment?** Sony Interactive Entertainment isn’t just the Best Place to Play — it’s also the Best Place to Work. Sony Interactive Entertainment (SIE) is the company behind the PlayStation brand. As a subsidiary of Sony Group Corporation, we’re part of a proud legacy of innovation and excellence. SIE is a dynamic technology company, delivering cutting\-edge hardware and network services to more than 100 million people and an entertainment leader, home to some of the most beloved and recognizable intellectual properties (IP) in the world. Our role at SIE is to create and nurture the experiences under the PlayStation brand, a name synonymous with entertainment excellence and creativity. **Role Overview:** The **People Technology Integration Engineer, Workday** is responsible for designing, building, and evolving integration and data solutions across SIE’s global Workday platform and broader HR technology ecosystem. This role operates as an independent contributor within People Technology Services, owning the design and delivery of scalable, secure, and reliable system integrations. The role focuses on enabling seamless data movement, system connectivity, and interoperability across platforms, ensuring that integrations are maintainable, performant, and aligned with architectural standards. Working within an Agile product delivery model, this role contributes to both the continuous evolution of integration capabilities and the stability of core data flows. The Integration Engineer partners closely with Platform Engineers, Product Managers, and Data teams to deliver cohesive, end\-to\-end technology solutions. This role is suited for individuals with strong technical depth who are motivated to build and improve integration architecture and data pipelines within an enterprise environment. **What you'll be doing:** **Integration Engineering \& Solution Design** * Design, build, and implement scalable integration solutions across the Workday platform and connected systems, including: * Workday Studio * Core Connectors and EIBs * API\-based integrations (REST/SOAP) * Define and apply integration design patterns that promote scalability, reusability, and maintainability * Design secure and reliable data flows, including transformation logic, orchestration, and error handling * Evaluate integration approaches and make informed design decisions, balancing business needs, system constraints, and long\-term sustainability * Own end\-to\-end delivery of integration solutions, including design, build, testing, and deployment readiness **Integration Architecture \& Platform Connectivity** * Contribute to the design and evolution of integration architecture across the People Technology ecosystem * Ensure consistent patterns for system connectivity, data exchange, and interface design * Document integration designs, data flows, and technical architecture to support maintainability and governance * Collaborate with multi\-functional teams to enable seamless end\-to\-end system interactions **Data Engineering \& Reporting Solutions** * Design and deliver data extracts, reporting solutions, and document generation capabilities (e.g., BIRT, Docs for Layouts) as part of the broader People data ecosystem * Develop and maintain calculated fields and data structures to support integration and reporting use cases * Ensure data accuracy, integrity, and consistency across integrations and downstream systems **Platform Reliability \& Operations** * Maintain reliability of integration pipelines through proactive monitoring, issue diagnosis, and resolution * Identify root causes of failures and implement structural improvements to enhance stability and performance * Optimize integrations for performance, scalability, and fault tolerance * Partner with team members to prioritize enhancements and manage competing demands **Delivery \& Agile Execution** * Translate business and technical requirements into well\-defined integration solutions * Participate actively in Agile delivery, including sprint planning, backlog refinement, and retrospectives * Own and deliver assigned workstreams within sprint cycles * Execute and support testing activities (unit, integration, UAT), ensuring end\-to\-end data validation * Collaborate with Platform Engineers and functional partners to deliver integrated, cross\-domain solutions **Governance, Security \& Quality** * Ensure all integration solutions meet data privacy, security, and audit requirements * Implement secure data exchange practices, including encryption, authentication, and secure file transfer (e.g., SFTP) * Follow established governance, release management, and change control practices * Ensure integration solutions align with platform architecture and engineering standards * Contribute to improving integration standards, documentation, and engineering practices **What we're looking for:** * 5\+ years of hands\-on experience with enterprise platforms, including design and delivery of Workday integrations * Demonstrated experience building and deploying integrations using: * Workday Studio * Core Connectors and EIBs * API\-based integrations (REST/SOAP) * Proven track record to design and troubleshoot integration solutions, including data transformation, interface logic, and failure handling * Experience implementing secure data exchange mechanisms, including encryption and secure file transfer * Hands\-on experience developing Workday reports (Advanced, Matrix, Composite) and calculated fields * Solid understanding of the Workday Core HCM data model and governance, release management, and change control practices * Contribute to improving documentation, standards, and ways of working across the platform **What we're looking for:** * 5\+ years of title: PhD Researcher in Sociology
description: Trinity is Ireland’s leading university and is ranked 75th in the world (QS World University Rankings 2026\). Founded in 1592, the University is steeped in history with a reputation for excellence in education, research, and innovation, which has been inspiring generations of thinkers for over 400 years. Thrive: Trinity College Dublin's Strategic Plan 2025 \- 2030 **Post status:** Fixed\-term Contract **Salary:** Maintenance stipend of €25,000 per annum, tax\-free, plus full tuition fees (EU/non\-EU) for 4 years **Post Summary** The candidate will join the PhD programme at the Department of Sociology of Trinity College Dublin, Ireland’s leading university which is located on a historic campus in the heart of Dublin. The Department will provide all logistic, academic and intellectual support and integrate the successful candidate into a vibrant doctoral programme at TCD. PhD students at the department are embedded in the interdisciplinary context of the School of Social Sciences and Philosophy which also includes economics, political science, and philosophy. PhD students in Sociology enjoy a wide range of training opportunities. For example, students participate in a research training programme in the department and have the opportunity to join masters level courses (including courses on research methods). Moreover, our PhD students are supported to attend high\-quality international workshops and summer schools. PhD students are expected to collaborate closely with their supervisors and will produce a PhD based on four papers of a publishable standard. PhD students may also have the opportunity to gain teaching experience by providing support within our undergraduate programme (up to 4 hours per week during term time, subject to availability). All teaching\-related work is paid separately in addition to the stipend. This PhD position forms part of an interdisciplinary research programme at Trinity College Dublin, (TCD). **Hereditary cardiac conditions are among the leading causes of sudden cardiac death (SCD) in children, adolescents, and young adults**. The abruptness of SCD in otherwise healthy individuals creates profound psychological, social, and cultural reverberations for surviving relatives, as well as the surrounding communities. These conditions represent both a clinical and human **challenge, situated at the intersection of physiology, identity, family life, and social relations**. This project responds to that complexity by adopting an interdisciplinary approach that integrates clinical cardiology, cellular biology, psychology and sociology, advanced biomedical engineering, and imaging technologies. **Application Procedure** Applicants should submit their application materials (motivation letter, CV, research statement, two referees, example of written work) in a single pdf indicating *“PhD Application in Sociology”* in the subject line to:\- **Dr. Camilla Devitt, Associate Professor in Department of Sociology** **devittca@tcd.ie** **Our Values \- putting our words into action** Values matter. They are key to the success of our Strategic Plan. We centre our behavioural values around the importance of belonging. We recognise that the university will thrive by each of us respecting and caring for one another. We know we can achieve much more through working together. We know too that we must take responsibility for our actions and be accountable. We must, and will never stop, imagining. And through being nature positive we connect not just with the environment but with our ambitions for a sustainable future. At Trinity, we are committed to equality, diversity, and inclusion. We welcome applications from all individuals, including those with disabilities, those who have followed non\-traditional career paths, and those who have taken time out for reasons such as family or caring responsibilities. We also welcome international applicants, including those who have been displaced due to conflict or war. Trinity College Dublin is Ireland’s premier university. We are an EU Sustainable Gender Equality Champion and hold an Athena Swan Silver Award, recognising our ongoing work to advance gender equality both within Trinity and across the higher education sector. Trinity is committed to supporting work–life balance and creating a family\-friendly working environment.

title: Planner
description: **Overview** At Sisk, we’re more than a construction company, we’re a family\-owned business with a global reach, committed to building excellence in every project. We deliver trust, certainty and value to our clients while embracing innovation and shaping the future of construction. The Planner supports project teams by developing, managing and monitoring programmes across design, procurement, construction, commissioning and handover stages. The role ensures projects are delivered safely, efficiently and in line with contractual, commercial and compliance requirements. Working collaboratively with project managers, engineers and commercial teams, the Planner provides accurate programme information, highlights risks and opportunities and supports decision\-making through robust reporting and analysis. **Responsibilities** **Programme Development \& Control** * Lead the development of baseline contract programmes, ensuring full logic links and a clear critical path. * Prepare construction target programmes that identify opportunities for efficiency and improvement. * Develop tender event programmes for subcontractor procurement, including design release and lead\-in activities. * Maintain and manage programme documentation within a Common Data Environment (CDE). * Create 4D models and/or 2D phasing plans to illustrate build sequences, logistics and phasing strategies. * Collaborate with project teams to develop construction methodologies that clearly demonstrate sequencing and safe delivery. * Support preparation of project preliminaries, including staff resource planning, logistics and temporary works. * Engage with the supply chain to incorporate design deliverables, lead\-in times, construction durations and resource requirements. * Identify programme risks and opportunities, incorporating mitigation strategies, float and time risk allowances. **Reporting, Progress \& As\-Built Information** * Develop and monitor tracking schedules to validate subcontractor programmes. * Produce as\-built programmes, highlighting deviations from the baseline. * Provide input into change requests, advising on programme impacts and supporting timely approvals. * Use 2D/4D phasing information to represent current programme status and progress. * Produce validated output rates to track live project progress and feed lessons learned into future tenders. * Undertake peer reviews of programmes to ensure quality, accuracy and consistency across projects. **Experience** **Essential** * Proven experience in planning within large\-scale construction projects. * Strong understanding of UK and EU legal frameworks, particularly contract management. * Proficiency in planning and programme software (e.g., Asta Powerproject). * Strong IT skills, including digital planning and reporting tools. * Ability to interpret complex project data, identify risks and propose solutions. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with experience collaborating across teams and with external stakeholders. * Experience working in multidisciplinary teams and contributing to project delivery. **Desirable** * Experience using Synchro and 4D planning tools to full potential. * Knowledge of BIM processes and collaborative data environments (CDEs). * Experience producing or reviewing 2D/4D phasing models. * Evidence of continuous professional development (e.g., CPD events, professional body engagement). * Experience mentoring or supporting junior colleagues. **Qualifications** **Essential** * Degree or diploma in Construction Management, Civil Engineering, Project Management or a related discipline. Equivalent professional experience will also be considered. * Certification or advanced training in planning software (e.g., Asta Powerproject). **Desirable** * Membership (or eligibility for membership) of a professional body such as CIOB, ICE or APM. * Advanced training in Asta Powerproject (progress analysis, S\-curve reporting, cost/resource management). Training in Synchro, BIM and collaborative data platforms. * Training in contract awareness (e.g., NEC, JCT). * Presentation skills training and/or leadership training. **Additional Information** * Competitive salary * 26 days annual leave * Pension and benefits * Unlimited learning and development opportunities * Much more **Our People and Recruitment Commitment** At Sisk Group our people are at the heart of our success. We offer the chance to work with purpose, build a career with no limits and be part of a great team. All vacancies are managed directly by our Internal Recruitment Team. Unsolicited contact or speculative CVs from unapproved agencies will not be accepted and no introductory fees will apply. We are an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, race, ethnicity, disability, age, sexual orientation, religion or any other protected characteristic. Reasonable adjustments are available during the recruitment process. We know the confidence gap and imposter phenomenon can stop talented candidates applying. You do not need to meet every criterion – your skills and potential matter. Don’t hold back we want to hear from you. All recruitment materials comply with legal and regulatory requirements including the EU Pay Transparency Directive and the Equality Acts UK and Ireland.

title: Primary Systems Analyst, Charles River Development, Officer
description: ****Company Profile:**** Charles River provides an end\-to\-end solution to automate front and middle office investment management functions across asset classes on a single platform. Delivered as a hosted service, the solution improves data quality and investment professional productivity, controls risk and lowers technology costs. Charles River serves more than 350 investment firms in over 40 countries in the institutional asset and fund management, private wealth, alternative investments, insurance, banking, and pension markets. Charles River were acquired by State Street in October 2018\. ****Background:**** A client\-facing, business and technical staff position responsible for all aspects of managing production Charles River IMS systems for multiple Clients as part of the Charles River Software as a Service (Saas) offerings which includes Application Management for hosted and non\-hosted clients. ****Key Responsibilities:**** * Act as Level 2 support for operational issues related to Charles River IMS applications, ensuring timely resolution of client cases logged in Salesforce. * Participate in regular client calls to provide updates, clarify queries, and maintain strong client relationships. * Manage Business Continuity (BC) and Disaster Recovery (DR) processes, including annual DR testing. * Configure and maintain interfaces for upstream/downstream data flows within CRD infrastructure and external entities; oversee connectivity, testing, and post\-go\-live support. * Own and monitor automated batch jobs, ensuring smooth execution and troubleshooting errors in nightly/daily processes. * Implement proactive monitoring for application errors and optimize batch performance to reduce runtime and chronic alerts. * Perform import/export troubleshooting, patching CRIMS applications, and production change deployments following CRD/STT change management processes (via ServiceNow). * Automate database refreshes from Production to Test and support on\-demand refresh requests. * Collaborate with CRD Professional Services, Product Support, and Engineering teams to address bugs, apply workarounds, and implement permanent fixes. * Support Self\-Service Portal (SSP) queries and maintain data archiving/purging processes to optimize production database performance. * Execute ad hoc and scheduled production changes (e.g., settlement day adjustments, exchange holiday calendar updates). * Provide support for lower environments for internal CRD usage and client testing prior to production deployment. * Lead incident management for Sev1 issues (e.g., batch failures, application outages), coordinate stakeholders, and prepare RCA \& Preventative Actions reports. * Join problem management calls and ensure case hygiene across all tickets. * Design, test, and deploy automated import/export/reporting processes tailored to client needs. ****Skills, Experience \& Qualifications Required:**** * Financial Services experience in Investment Management, Trading, or related systems (Charles River IMS preferred). * Strong knowledge of MS SQL Server, Oracle, and/or Sybase database configuration and administration. * Familiarity with servers, operating systems, networks, scripting, XML, programming languages, job scheduling, system monitoring, and DR/BC processes. * Excellent customer service, troubleshooting, and communication skills (verbal and written). * Ability to collaborate across internal teams and external vendors to deliver seamless client service. * Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, IT, Financial Engineering, Engineering, Economics, Mathematics, or related discipline preferred. * Minimum 3\+ years of relevant industry experience. ****About State Street**** **What we do.** State Street is one of the largest custodian banks, asset managers and asset intelligence companies in the world. From technology to product innovation we're making our mark on the financial services industry. For more than two centuries, we've been helping our clients safeguard and steward the investments of millions of people. And our wealth of development programs and educational support will help you reach your full potential. **Inclusion, Diversity, and Social Responsibility.** We truly believe our employees' diverse backgrounds, experience and perspective are a powerful contributor to creating an inclusive environment where everyone can thrive and reach their maximum potential while adding value to both our organization and our clients. State Street is an equal opportunity and affirmative action employer. Company: Charles River Development Discover more at www.StateStreet.com/careers ****About State Street**** Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success. We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work\-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. title: Product Analyst
description: **Base Location:** You'll be expected to spend 50% of your working week in one of the following locations : Perth, Reading, Dublin, Havant or Cardiff **Salary:** £49,004 \- £57,728 and a range of benefits to support your finances, wellbeing and family. **Working Pattern:** 12 month FTC \| Full Time \| Flexible First options available **The Role** As Product Analyst you will play a pivotal role in shaping the direction of our IT products. You will collaborate with cross\-functional teams including business stakeholders, engineers, delivery leads to ensure the successful development, implementation, and optimization of solutions **You Will** * Conduct in\-depth analysis of business requirements and collaborate with stakeholders to define clear and detailed specifications. * Work closely with cross\-functional teams, including engineers, data team, delivery lead and business stakeholders to ensure seamless integration of products into existing systems and processes. * Collect and analyse user feedback to refine products features and functionalities, ensuring a user\-centric approach and continuous improvement. * Ensure successful delivery of products using Agile methodologies. **You Have** * Experience with Agile methodologies and proficiency in Business Process Mapping. * Understanding of Agile software development practices, including Scrum and Kanban frameworks. * Outstanding coordination abilities, with a track record of collaborating with both internal and external stakeholders in business and technical domains. * A degree level qualification or relevant experience with well\-developed communication skills with the ability to present and offer challenge to internal and external audiences on business critical content. * Experience in supporting team facilitation and management tools, e.g. Azure DevOps/ Jira **About SSE** SSE’s purpose is to provide energy needed today while building a better world of energy for tomorrow. We do this by developing, building, operating and investing in electricity infrastructure and businesses needed in the energy transition. Our Transforming for Growth investment plan sees us investing £33bn in critical electricity infrastructure across the five years to 2030\. Our IT division powers growth across all SSE business areas by making sure we have the systems, software and security needed to take the lead in a low carbon world. They provide expertise, advice and day\-to\-day support in emerging technologies, data and analytics, cyber security and more. **Flexible Benefits To Fit Your Life** Enjoy discounts on private healthcare and gym memberships. Wellbeing benefits like a free online GP and 24/7 counselling service. Interest\-free loans on tech and transport season tickets, or a new bike with our Cycle to Work scheme. As well as generous family entitlements such as maternity and adoption pay, and paternity leave. **Work with an equal opportunity employer** SSE will make any reasonable adjustments you need to ensure that your application and experience with us is positive. Please contact David.brickell@sse.com / 01738 275 846 to discuss how we can support you. We're dedicated to fostering an open and inclusive workplace where people from all backgrounds can thrive. We create equal opportunities for everyone to succeed and especially welcome applications from those who may not be well represented in our workforce or industry. **Ready to apply?** Start your online application using the Apply Now box on this page. We only accept applications made online. We'll be in touch after the closing date to let you know if we'll be taking your application further. If you're offered a role with SSE, you'll need to complete a criminality check and a credit check before you start work.
